Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ (۲)
The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-knowing, (2)
غَافِرِ ٱلذَّنۢبِ وَقَابِلِ ٱلتَّوْبِ شَدِيدِ ٱلْعِقَابِ ذِى ٱلطَّوْلِ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ إِلَيْهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۳)
forgiver of sins and acceptor of repentance, severe in retribution, [yet] all-bountiful; there is no god except Him, [and] toward Him is the destination. (3)

Key Points

Based on some narrations, the disconnected letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah) have an interpretation that no one knows except Allah. (2) However, based on some other narrations (considering the verses following these letters, which relate to the revelation of the Qur'an), Allah argues with His opponents, stating that He composed the Qur'an from these very letters of the alphabet that are available to you; if you can, bring forth something similar to it.

"Dhī al-Ṭawl" (ذى الطول) has two meanings: Possessor of grace and bounty, which symbolizes Divine mercy, and Possessor of power, which symbolizes Divine wrath.

Verses that begin with "Tanzīl al-Kitāb" (تنزیل الكتاب) mention the name of Allah along with His attributes, including: «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز الحكیم» (3) «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز العلیم» (4) «تنزیل الكتاب من الرّحمن الرّحیم» (5) «تنزیل العزیز الرّحیم» (6) «تنزیل من حكیم حمید» (7) «تنزیل من ربّ العالمین» (8)

Therefore, the source of revelation is the Possessor of power, wisdom, mercy, Lordship (Rubūbiyyah), and is worthy of praise and commendation.

Forgiveness in the Qur'an

The Holy Qur'an mentions factors for forgiveness, including:

Faith. «آمنّا بربّنا لیغفر لنا» (9) Piety (Taqwa). «اِن تتّقوا اللّه... یغفر لكم» (10) Following the Prophets. «فاتّبعونى... یغفر لكم» (11) Pardoning and overlooking people's faults. «و لیعفوا.... یغفراللّه لكم» (12) Spending (infaq) on people. «ان تقرضوا اللّه... یغفر لكم» (13) Jihad. «تجاهدون فى سبیل اللّه... یغفر لكم» (14) Worship. «أنِ اعبدوا اللّه... یغفر لكم» (15) Avoiding major sins (Kabā'ir). «اِن تجتنبوا كبائر.... نكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم» (16) A person's own supplication, seeking forgiveness (Istighfār), and repentance (Inābah). «ظلمتُ نفسى... فغفر له» (17) The supplication of Allah's chosen servants (Awliyā') for a person. «یا أبانا استغفر لنا...» (18)

2) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān. 3) Az-Zumar, 1. 4) Ghafir, 2. 5) Fussilat, 3. 6) Ya Sin, 5. 7) Fussilat, 42. 8) Al-Waqi'ah, 80. 9) Ta Ha, 73. 10) Al-Anfal, 29. 11) Al 'Imran, 31. 12) An-Nur, 22. 13) At-Taghabun, 17. 14) As-Saff, 11 - 12. 15) Nuh, 3 - 4. 16) An-Nisa, 31. 17) Al-Qasas, 16. 18) Yusuf, 97.

Messages

1- A novel method of presenting a subject is an incentive for listening. «حم تنزیل»

2- The Qur'an was revealed gradually. ("Tanzīl" (تنزیل) means gradual revelation).

3- The grandeur of the Speaker should not prevent the discourse from being revealed at the level of the audience's comprehension. (Allah, the Exalted (Al-Azīz), reveals His word.) «تنزیل... من اللّه العزیز»

4- The word of Allah, the Exalted, is also exalted and impenetrable, and no logic can break it. «تنزیل الكتاب... العزیز»

5- The Qur'an is a manifestation of Allah's Exaltation (Izzah) and Knowledge. «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز العلیم»

6- The Qur'an is a means for the exaltation and awareness of Muslims. «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز العلیم»

7- The revelation of the Book, the declaration of forgiveness, and the Divine warning are all aimed at human perfection and reaching Allah. «تنزیل - غافر - شدید - الیه المصیر»

8- The revelation of the Book is for recognizing the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ād). «تنزیل الكتاب ... لا اله الاّ هو الیه المصیر»

9- Benefiting from Divine favors is achieved under the shadow of the Heavenly Book. «تنزیل الكتاب - غافر الذنب»

10- Both the Book (law) and accountability are necessary, as is forgiveness or punishment in its proper place. «تنزیل - غافر الذنب - شدید العقاب»

11- Forgiveness from powerful and victorious individuals is beautiful. (Pardoning belongs to the great ones.) «العزیز العلیم غافر الذنب»

12- No one can prevent the revelation from descending, because He is impenetrable, «من اللّه العزیز», and He knows upon whom to reveal it. «العلیم»

13- The path introduced by Islam originates from Allah and leads back to Allah. «من اللّه العزیز... الیه المصیر»

14- Allah's Mercy precedes His Wrath. «غافر الذنب... شدید العقاب»

15- Sin is sometimes forgiven through good deeds. «غافر الذنب» (Similar to the verse «انّ الحسنات یذهبن السیئات» (19)) and sometimes through repentance. «قابل التوب»

16- A believer must be between fear and hope, because Allah is both the Forgiver and the Punisher. «غافر الذنب... شدید العقاب»

17- Divine Mercy and Wrath must be viewed together, so that neither despair nor arrogance arises. «غافر الذنب... شدید العقاب»

18- Divine punishment is due to our own actions; otherwise, Allah continuously bestows favor. «شدید العقاب ذى الطول»

19- Allah's favor is perpetual. «ذى الطول»

19) Hud, 114.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ghafir

This Surah has eighty-five verses and is Makki (Meccan). The name of this Surah, "Ghafir," is taken from the third verse, which introduces Allah as «غافر الذنب» (The Forgiver of Sin).

The other name of this Surah is "Mu'min" (The Believer), due to the person who was in Pharaoh's court and concealed his faith so that he could help Moses. The story of this individual, who became known as the "Believer from the Family of Pharaoh" («مؤمن آل فرعون»), is mentioned in verses 28 onwards of this Surah.

The content of this Surah, besides the stories of Moses, Noah, 'Ad, and Thamud, includes the teachings of Monotheism (Tawhīd), the Resurrection (Ma'ād), and Divine Wisdom. Approximately twenty verses concern the Believer from the Family of Pharaoh, which are not found elsewhere in the Qur'an.

In the Qur'an, seven consecutive Surahs begin with the verse «حم» (Ha Mim). This Surah is the first of them, and the rest are: Fussilat, Shura, Zukhruf, Dukhan, Jathiyah, and Ahqaf. They are sometimes referred to as the "Hawāmīm."

In narrations, reciting the "Hawāmīm" Surahs during the night prayer (Salāt al-Layl) is recommended, and it is narrated from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that the "Hawāmīm" are the core of the Qur'an.

We read in a Hadith: The "Hawāmīm" are the crown of the Qur'an and its fragrant flowers. Allah bestows His favor upon the reciter of these Surahs, his neighbors, relatives, and friends, and the Throne ('Arsh) and the Pedestal (Kursī) seek forgiveness for him. (1)

1) Tafsir Namūneh.

Ayah

مَا يُجَـٰدِلُ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَلَا يَغْرُرْكَ تَقَلُّبُهُمْ فِى ٱلْبِلَـٰدِ (٤)
No one disputes the signs of Allah except the faithless. So do not be misled by their bustle in the towns. (4)

Key Points

Jadal (جدل) literally means twisting a rope. In technical terms, it refers to discussions where the parties seek to defeat each other through the twists and turns of the argument. Debate (Jidal) is of two types:

A) Good Jidal, which is the practice (sirah) of the Prophets and is a type of discussion and effort aimed at guiding people. Just as the disbelievers said to Prophet Noah (عليه السلام): "O Noah, you have disputed with us and multiplied your dispute." «یا نوح قد جادلتنا فاكثرت جدالنا»(20)

B) False Jidal, which introduces various misleading and false schemes with the aim of erasing or diminishing the truth. «و جادلوا بالباطل لیدحضوا به الحقّ»(21)

In scholarly and doctrinal matters, one must avoid Jidal except through the best method: «و لا تجادلوا اهل الكتاب الاّ بالّتى هى احسن»(22) «جادلهم بالتى هى احسن»(23)

Examples of Good Jidal

  • Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) invited Nimrod to worship God and said: "Worship the God who holds death and life in His hands." Nimrod said: "Death and life are in my hands too. I can order an execution or free a condemned person." Abraham said: "Allah has designed the system such that the sun rises from the East; you make the sun appear from the West." Nimrod was dumbfounded.(24)
  • The Holy Quran has repeatedly stated in discussions with opponents that if you bring a book like Me, or ten chapters like My chapters, or one chapter like Me, I will withdraw My call. It even told them to invite anyone they wish for cooperation, but know that you will never bring even one chapter like the Quran.(25)

False Jidal is instigated by Satan. «انّ الشیاطین لیوحون الى اولیائهم لیجادلوكم»(26)

Harms of Jidal:

  1. It is a cause of deviation. «ما ضلّ قومٌ بعد هدىً كانوا علیه الاّ اوتوا الجَدَل»(27) No guided group deviated except because they were given to undue disputes (Jadal).
  2. It is a cause of Divine wrath. «من جادل فى خصومة بغیر علم لم یزل فى سخط اللّه حتّى ینزع»(28) Whoever engages in Jadal in a dispute without knowledge remains under the wrath of Allah until he ceases the dispute.
  3. It leads to regret. «ایّاك و اللجاجة فان اوّلها جهل و آخرها الندامة»(29) Avoid obstinacy (lajajah), which is the basis of Jidal, for its beginning is ignorance and its end is regret.

Method of Good Discussion and Jidal

  1. Ask the opponent for proof. «قل هاتوا برهانكم»(30)
  2. Do not consider oneself superior from the start. «انّا او ایّاكم لعلى هدى او فى ضلال مبین»(31)
  3. Accept what is true, even if one rejects it due to expediency. «فیهما اثم كبیر و منافع للناس و اثمهما اكبر»(32)
  4. Give the opponent time to think. «و ان احد من المشركین استجارك فاجره»(33)
  5. Observe courtesy and composure. «لا تسبّوا الذّین یدعون من دون اللّه عدوّاً»(34)
  6. Be fair and do not view everyone equally. «فریق منهم»(35)
  7. Speak logically. «قولاً سدیدا»(36)
  8. Speak softly. «قولاً لیّنا»(37)

Messages

  1. Disputing the truth has no cause other than disbelief. «ما یجادل... الاّ الذّین كفروا»
  2. Do not expect everyone to accept your truthful statement. «یجادل فى آیات اللّه»
  3. The method of the disbelievers is false Jidal. (The present tense verb «ما یجادل» indicates continuity.)
  4. Even the Prophets need reminder and admonition. «فلا یغررك...»
  5. Do not be intimidated by the disbelievers and do not take a passive stance. «فلا یغررك تقلّبهم»
  6. The movements of the disbelievers are a respite that Allah has granted them until their measure is full; do not worry. «فلا یغررك»
  7. The disbelievers are striving; do not be negligent. «تقلّبهم فى البلاد» (Military maneuvers, political conferences, periodic travels, and international treaties held by the leaders of arrogance are examples of «تقلّبهم فى البلاد».)
  8. The leader of the Islamic society must be aware of the movements of the disbelievers. «تقلّبهم فى البلاد»

Ayah

كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَٱلْأَحْزَابُ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۖ وَهَمَّتْ كُلُّ أُمَّةٍۭ بِرَسُولِهِمْ لِيَأْخُذُوهُ ۖ وَجَـٰدَلُوا۟ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ لِيُدْحِضُوا۟ بِهِ ٱلْحَقَّ فَأَخَذْتُهُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عِقَابِ (٥)
The people of Noah denied before them and the [heathen] factions [who came] after them. Every nation attempted to lay hands on their apostle, and disputed erroneously to refute the truth. Then I seized them; so how was My retribution?! (5)

Key Points

In Ayah 12 of Surah Sad, the names of the parties (Ahzab) and nations after Prophet Nuh (عليه السلام) are mentioned as follows: «كذّبت قبلهم قوم نوح و عاد و فرعون ذوالاتاد و ثمود و قوم لوط و اصحاب الایكة اولئك الاحزاب»

Messages

1- Awareness of the difficulties faced by others is the key to solace and steadfastness. «كذّبت قبلهم...»

2- The formation and multiplicity of parties (Ahzab) is not a sign of righteousness (Haqqaniyyah). «كذّبت... الاحزاب»

3- The agenda of the parties opposing the Prophets consists of: Denial (Takdhib). «كذّبت قبلهم» Conspiracy. «همّت كلّ امّة برسولهم» Malicious propaganda. «جادلوا بالباطل لیدحضوا به الحقّ»

4- Life in the early stages of history was tribal, but gradually became organized (institutionalized). «قوم نوح و الاحزاب من بعدهم»

5- The opponents of the Prophets were sometimes tyrants (Taghuts), «فرعون», sometimes tribes, «قومهم», and sometimes organizations (coalitions). «احزاب»

6- Allah is the Protector of the Prophets. «كذبت قبلهم قوم... فكیف كان عقاب»

7- All opponents had one common goal in their struggle against the heavenly leaders. «همّت كلّ امّة برسولهم لیأخذوه»

8- Opponents are not satisfied with anything less than the killing of the heavenly leader, «لیأخذوه», and the destruction of the truth (Haqq), «لیدحضوا به الحقّ».

9- The opponents are dealing directly with Allah. «اخذتهم فكیف كان عقاب» The lamp that the Almighty (Izad) ignites, Whoever attempts to blow it out, his own beard shall burn.

10- Divine punishments sometimes occur in this world. «فاخذتهم»

Ayahs

وَكَذَٰلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ (٦)
That is how the word of your Lord became due concerning the faithless, that they shall be inmates of the Fire. (6)
ٱلَّذِينَ يَحْمِلُونَ ٱلْعَرْشَ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُۥ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَيُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ رَبَّنَا وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ رَّحْمَةً وَعِلْمًا فَٱغْفِرْ لِلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ سَبِيلَكَ وَقِهِمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۷)
Those who bear the Throne, and those who are around it, celebrate the praise of their Lord and have faith in Him, and they plead for forgiveness for the faithful: ‘Our Lord! You embrace all things in mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your way and save them from the punishment of hell. (7)

Key Points

In the previous verses, we read that people divided into two groups in response to the invitation of the prophets: one group receives forgiveness, and the other faces Divine wrath. This verse describes the prayer of the angels and their support for the first group.

What is the Throne (al-Arsh)? The Throne of Allah is mentioned twenty times in the Qur'an. Linguistically, ‘Arsh (عرش) is a high-legged throne, and Kursī (كرسىّ) is a low-legged throne. When we read: «وسع كرسیّه السموات و الارض» (His Kursi encompasses the heavens and the earth), His Kursi encompasses existence, so what must His Throne be like?

The Throne (al-Arsh) is either a metaphor for the center of Divine power or the center for the issuance of Divine decrees. The intent of the "bearers of the Throne" (حاملان عرش) are angels whose number is stated to be eight. «یحمل عرش ربّك فوقهم یومئذ ثمانیة»(38)

It must be said that the reality of the Throne is not clear to us, but perhaps from the totality of the verses, one can understand that the universe has a center, and Allah has complete dominance over that center. «استوى على العرش» (He settled upon the Throne), and the execution of the Divine will occurs through the angels who are around this command center.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Throne is the knowledge that Allah has made His prophets and messengers aware of. «العرش هو العلم الذّى اطلع اللّه علیه انبیائه و رسله»(39)

Alongside the word 'Throne,' verses are presented, the summation of which leads man to the command center, such as: «استوى على العرش» (He settled upon the Throne), «یغشى الّیل النّهار... له الخلق و الامر»(40) (He covers the night with the day... to Him belong the creation and the command), «یدبّر الامر»(41) (He regulates the affair), «... یفصّل الآیات»(42) (He details the signs), «یعلم ما یلج فى الارض و ما یخرج منها و ما ینزل من السماء و ما یعرج فیها»(43) (He knows what penetrates the earth and what emerges from it, and what descends from the heaven and what ascends into it).

The rotation of night and day, the regulation of affairs, the detailing of signs, and the awareness of every particle of existence, and what descends from the heaven and what ascends to the heaven, is the secret that all these matters occur in the Divine Throne.

The late Allamah Tabataba'i says: The Throne is a reality among realities, an external affair, and the center for regulating the affairs of the world.(44)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Throne and the Kursi are two of the greatest gates to the unseen world. «العرش و الكرسى بابان من اكبر ابواب الغیوب»

The intent of the Throne is not a physical seat, because a seat cannot be established upon water. «و كان عرشه على الماء»(45) Rather, the intent is that on the day when the earth and the heavens did not yet exist and the world was entirely water, the domain of Allah's command was over the water, and after the creation of the heavens and the earth, the headquarters for commanding the universe was transferred to the heavens.

38) Al-Haqqah, 7. 39) Ma‘ani al-Akhbar, p. 29. 40) Al-A‘raf, 54. 41) Yunus, 3. 42) Ar-Ra‘d, 2. 43) Al-Hadid, 4. 44) Tafsir al-Mizan. 45) Hud, 7.

Messages

1- Allah's wrath upon the disbelievers is one of the Divine traditions. «كذلك حقّت كلمت ربّك»

2- Divine punishment is not arbitrary; rather, it is based on justice and truth. «حقّت كلمت ربّك»

3- The retribution and punishment of criminals are among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «حقّت كلمت ربّك»

4- The factor leading to the destruction of past nations was disbelief (kufr). «حقّت كلمت ربّك على الذّین كفروا»

5- Believers should be proud that those close to the Divine presence constantly pray for them. «یحملون العرش و یستغفرون»

6- Islam elevates human thought from food, clothing, and shelter to the Throne, angels, glorification (tasbīh), and praise (taḥmīd). «یحملون العرش - یسبّحون»

7- Only Allah is infinite; even the Divine Throne has limits. «حوله»

8- Praise (Ḥamd) and glorification (Tasbīh) are mentioned together many times in the Qur'an, and they are paired in the remembrance during Rukū‘ (bowing) and Sujūd (prostration). «یسبّحون بحمد ربّهم»

9- The etiquette of supplication (Du'a) is: first, glorification and praise, «یسبحون بحمد ربّهم»; then, magnification and description, «وسعت كل شى ء رحمة و علما»; and finally, the supplication itself. «فاغفر»

10- The weight of responsibility should not prevent you from glorifying and praising Allah and remembering those in need. The angels bearing the Throne are both mindful of Allah, «یسبّحون بحمد ربّهم», and pray for those in need. «یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

11- Affinity is necessary in supplication. Angels who possess faith pray for the believers. «یؤمنون... یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

12- Faith is the connecting link and communication between the physical world (Alam al-Mulk) and the spiritual world (Alam al-Malakūt). «یؤمنون به و یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

13- The seeking of forgiveness (Istighfār) by certain individuals on behalf of others is effective only if they themselves are people of faith. «یؤمنون به و یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

14- There is a relationship between angels and humans. (Imam Sajjad عليه السلام sends prayers and salutations to groups of angels in the Sahifah Sajjadiyyah, and) the angels bearing the Throne send salutations to humans in this verse. «یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

15- Praying for others is a commendable and recognized Qur'anic value. «و یستغفرون للذّین آمنوا»

16- Those who are included in the angels' prayers are people of faith and action. «آمنوا... اتبعوا»

17- Repentance (Tawbah) alone is not sufficient; practical adherence is necessary. «تابوا و اتبعوا»

18- The path to salvation lies in following the way of Allah. «و اتبعوا سبیلك و قهم عذاب الجحیم»

Ayah

رَبَّنَا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ٱلَّتِى وَعَدتَّهُمْ وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ ءَابَآئِهِمْ وَأَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۸)
Our Lord! Admit them into the Gardens of Eden, which You have promised them, along with whoever is righteous among their forebears, their spouses and their descendants. Indeed You are the All-mighty, the All-wise. (8)

Key Points

Regarding the togetherness of family members, all of whom are destined for Paradise, we read elsewhere in the Quran: «الحقنا بهم ذرّیاتهم»(46) (We will join their believing descendants with them) We will join the believing generation with their ancestors in Paradise. The Prophets and the angels used to begin their supplications (Du'as) with «ربّنا» (Our Lord) and «ربّ» (Lord):

The supplication of Prophet Adam: «ربّنا ظلمنا انفسنا»(47) The supplication of Prophet Noah: «ربّ اغفرلى ولوالدى»(48) The supplication of Prophet Abraham: «ربّ اجعل هذا بلداً آمنا»(49) The supplication of Prophet Joseph: «ربّ قد آتیتنى من الملك»(50) The supplication of Prophet Moses: «ربّ بما أنعمت علىّ من خیر فقیر»(51) The supplication of Prophet Solomon: «ربّ أوزعنى ان اشكر نعمتك»(52) The supplication of Prophet Jesus: «ربّنا أنزل علینا مائدة»(53) The supplication of the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله: «ربّ أعوذ بك من همزات الشیاطین»(54) The supplication of the believers: «ربّنا ما خلقت هذا باطلا فقنا عذاب النار»(55) The supplication of the angels in this Ayah, where they say: «ربّنا و ادخلهم جنّات»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُنَادَوْنَ لَمَقْتُ ٱللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ مِن مَّقْتِكُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ إِذْ تُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ فَتَكْفُرُونَ (۱٠)
It will be proclaimed to the faithless: ‘Surely, Allah’s outrage [towards you] is greater than your outrage towards yourselves, as you were invited to faith, but you disbelieved.’ (10)

Key Points

The word "yunādūn" (ینادَون) is used twice in the Quran, both times referring to the inhabitants of Hellfire and serving to humiliate them. One is this Ayah, and the other is Ayah 44 of Surah Fussilat: "yunādūn min makānin ba‘īd" (ینادَون من مكان بعید). In the preceding verses, the prayer of the angels for the believers was mentioned, and this Ayah speaks of the Divine wrath concerning the disbelievers. There, the discussion was about the vastness of Divine Mercy: "wa si‘at kulla shay’in raḥmatan" (وسعت كل شى ءٍ رحمةً), and here, the discussion is about the great wrath: "la maqtu Allāhi akbar" (لمقت اللّه اكبر). Yes, both His Mercy and His Wrath are great.

Messages

1- In Hellfire, besides the physical fire, there are also psychological torments and humiliation. "yunādūn la maqtu Allāhi akbar" (ینادون لمقت اللّه اكبر)

2- If we commit disbelief today, tomorrow we will incur wrath against ourselves. "maqtukum anfusakum" (مقتكم انفسكم)

3- Allah completes the argument (provides conclusive proof). "idh tud‘awna ilā al-īmān" (اذ تُدعون الى الایمان)

4- The source of the punishment for the inhabitants of Hellfire is disbelief (kufr). "fa takfurūn" (فتكفرون)

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَمَتَّنَا ٱثْنَتَيْنِ وَأَحْيَيْتَنَا ٱثْنَتَيْنِ فَٱعْتَرَفْنَا بِذُنُوبِنَا فَهَلْ إِلَىٰ خُرُوجٍ مِّن سَبِيلٍ (۱۱)
They will say, ‘Our Lord! Twice did You make us die, and twice did You give us life. We admit our sins. Is there any way out [from this plight]?’ (11)

Key Points

The apparent meaning of the two deaths is death at the end of life and death at the end of Barzakh (the intermediate realm), and the meaning of the two resurrections (being brought to life) is being brought to life in Barzakh and being brought to life on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). Although some have said: the meaning of the two deaths is one being the time before the creation of man and the other being the end of his life, and the meaning of the two resurrections is the time of man's creation in this world and the time of the Day of Judgment. They have cited as proof for their statement the Ayah, «كنتم امواتا فاحیاكم ثم یمیتكم ثم یحییكم»(62), but what was stated is better, because the Ayah discusses causing death (ميراندن), not being dead before birth.

The heartbreaking desire of the dwellers of Hell for return to the world has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran: Sometimes they say: Is there any way to return? Sometimes they wish they could return to the world and become righteous doers. And sometimes they desperately say: Our Lord! Return us to the world so that we may perform righteous deeds. The following Ayahs express these realities:

«هل الى مردّ من سبیل»(63) Is there any way of return? «لو انّ لى كرّةً فاكون من المحسنین»(64) If only I had a return [to life], I would be among the doers of good. «ربّنا اخرجنا منها فان عدنا فانا ظالمون»(65) Our Lord, remove us from it; if we return [to sin], then indeed, we will be wrongdoers. «ربّ ارجعون لعلّى اعمل صالحاً»(66) My Lord, send me back, that I might do righteousness. «فارجعنا نعمل صالحا»(67) So return us [to the world] that we may do righteousness.

Ayah

ذَٰلِكُم بِأَنَّهُۥٓ إِذَا دُعِىَ ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُۥ كَفَرْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن يُشْرَكْ بِهِۦ تُؤْمِنُوا۟ ۚ فَٱلْحُكْمُ لِلَّهِ ٱلْعَلِىِّ ٱلْكَبِيرِ (۱۲)
[They will be told,] ‘This [plight of yours] is because, when Allah was invoked alone, you would disbelieve, but if partners were ascribed to Him you would believe. So the judgment belongs to Allah, the All-exalted, the All-great.’ (12)

Key Points

Many are those who are not pure believers, and alongside Islam and faith, they are also attached to powers (worldly authorities). In their speech and actions, while accepting Islam, they pay attention to gaining the satisfaction of this person and that person, and observing the laws of the East and the West. In the gatherings of the disbelievers, a kind of weakness, humiliation, loneliness, and shame casts a shadow over them, and sometimes the matter reaches the point where they do not perform the prayer (Salat) because of their presence in a formal meeting.

Messages

1- When punishing the criminals, state the reason for their downfall and the cause of Your wrath. «ذلكم بانه اذا...» 2- Disbelief (Kufr) is the reason for perpetual torment. «هل الى خروج من سبیل ذلكم بانه... كفرتم» 3- Polytheism (Shirk) is disbelief (Kufr). «دُعِىَ اللّه وحده كفرتم» 4- Judgment on the Day of Resurrection belongs to Allah. «فالحكم للّه» 5- The beings whom you consider His partners are never comparable to Him, because He is «العلىّ الكبیر» (The Most High, The Most Great). 6- Do not think that polytheism harms Allah. «انّ الشرك... فالحكم للّه العلى الكبیر»

Ayahs

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَيُنَزِّلُ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ رِزْقًا ۚ وَمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ إِلَّا مَن يُنِيبُ (۱۳)
It is He who shows you His signs and sends down provision for you from the sky. Yet no one takes admonition except those who return penitently [to Allah]. (13)
فَٱدْعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۱٤)
So supplicate Allah, putting exclusive faith in Him, though the faithless should be averse. (14)

Key Points

The previous verses spoke of polytheism (shirk) and its punishment, and this verse invites people to monotheism (Tawhid). In this verse, He says: only those who turn (in anābah) remember (or take heed), and in Ayah 19 of Surah Ar-Ra'd, He said: only the wise remember: «انّما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب» Therefore, the truly wise are those who turn (in anābah), and those who truly turn (in anābah) are the wise. Yes, tears must be based on knowledge (ma'rifah). «ترى اعینهم تفیض من الدمع مما عرفوا من الحقّ»(69)

The phrase «ولو كره الكافرون» appears three times in the Qur'an, and the phrases «ولو كره المشركون» and «ولو كره المجرمون» appear twice each. This indicates that on our path, we should not seek to gain the satisfaction of the disbelievers (kāfirūn), the polytheists (mushrikūn), and the criminals (mujrimūn), nor should we abandon our duty because of their dissatisfaction.

3- The source of sustenance (rizq) is the sky. The sun, clouds, and atmosphere provide the light, warmth, rain, and oxygen necessary for life. «من السماء رزقاً»

5- First knowledge (recognition), then action. «یریكم آیاته... فادعوا...»

6- Your sincere prayer and expression of need lead to your growth, not to the greatness of God. «العلى الكبیر هو الذّى یریكم... فادعوا اللّه مخلصین»

8- No theory, law, preference, inclination, custom, tradition, behavior of ancestors or others, and no threat, propaganda, or temptation should affect your monotheistic religion. «مخلصین له الدین»

9- Only Allah is worthy of prayer and supplication. «فادعوا اللّه»

10- Sincerity (Ikhlās) is the condition for the acceptance of prayer. «فادعوا اللّه مخلصین»

11- We should not be concerned with satisfying the disbelievers (kuffār) and those with differing views. «ولو كره الكافرون»

Ayahs

رَفِيعُ ٱلدَّرَجَـٰتِ ذُو ٱلْعَرْشِ يُلْقِى ٱلرُّوحَ مِنْ أَمْرِهِۦ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ لِيُنذِرَ يَوْمَ ٱلتَّلَاقِ (۱٥)
Raiser of ranks, Lord of the Throne, He casts the Spirit of His command upon whomever of His servants that He wishes, that he may warn [people] of the Day of Encounter. (15)
يَوْمَ هُم بَـٰرِزُونَ ۖ لَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مِنْهُمْ شَىْءٌ ۚ لِّمَنِ ٱلْمُلْكُ ٱلْيَوْمَ ۖ لِلَّهِ ٱلْوَٰحِدِ ٱلْقَهَّارِ (۱٦)
The day when they will emerge [from their graves], nothing about them will be hidden from Allah. ‘To whom does the sovereignty belong today?’ ‘To Allah, the One, the All-paramount!’ (16)

Key Points

Elevation and greatness are of two types: a) Spatial (locational), like the Ayah «یرفع ابراهیم القواعد»(70), where raising the foundations of the Ka'bah is spatial. b) Status (positional), like «یرفع اللّه الذّین آمنوا... درجات»(71). «رفیع الدرجات» can be interpreted in two ways: One is that Allah possesses high ranks and status, and the supreme position. The other is that Allah raises the ranks of people according to their merit, similar to the Ayah «یرفع اللّه الذّین آمنوا» and the Ayah «رفع بعضكم فوق بعض»(72), which means that Allah gives superiority to some over others because of their performance. The meaning of Ruh (Spirit) is either the revelation itself, which causes the spiritual life of society, or the angel commissioned with the revelation. In other instances, we read: «و كذلك اوحینا الیك روحاً من امرنا»(73) and «ینزل الملائكة بالروح من امره على من یشاء من عباده ان انذروا»(74). Since the Quran and revelation are named Ruh (Spirit), «یلقى الروح», it becomes clear that revelation is the cause of life for people, and the prophets invite people to spiritual life. «دعاكم لما یحییكم»(75). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Day of Resurrection is the day of meeting between the inhabitants of the heavens and the inhabitants of the earth.(76) The Sender of the revelation is Allah, the Possessor of the Throne. The Receiver of the revelation are the sincere servants whom the Wise God chooses. «من یشاء من عباده». The goal and subject of the revelation is warning (admonition). «لینذر». The intermediary of the revelation is a specific angel. «یلقى الروح». We read in narrations: The question «لمن الملك» (Whose is the Sovereignty?) is from Allah, and the answer «للّه الواحد القهار» (To Allah, the One, the Irresistible) is from the prophets and the Divine saints.(77) The manifestation and appearance of people on the Day of Resurrection, and their presence before the Divine Presence in a manner that leaves no point of ambiguity for just judgment, is due to the following matters: a) The leveling of the earth and the removal of mountains. «قاعاً صَفصفا»(78). b) The emergence of everyone from the graves. «و اذ القبور بعثرت»(79), «و أخرجت الارض اثقالها»(80). c) The unfolding of the record of deeds. «و اذا الصّحف نُشرت»(81). d) The embodiment of deeds. «ینظر المرء ما قَدّمت یداه»(82). e) The uncovering of hidden matters. «بدالهم ما كانوا یخفون من قبل»(83). f) The witnessing of body parts. «تشهد أرجلهم»(84).

Ayah

ٱلْيَوْمَ تُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ ۚ لَا ظُلْمَ ٱلْيَوْمَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۱۷)
‘Today every soul shall be requited for what it has earned. There will be no injustice today. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning.’ (17)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah and this Ayah, six points regarding the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) have been raised: A) The presence of all people. «هم بارزون» B) The revelation of all deeds and thoughts. «لا یخفى على اللّه شى ء» C) The manifestation of the power of the Truth (Allah). «لمن الملك الیوم للّه الواحد القهّار» D) Everyone receiving their due recompense. «تجزى كل نفس» E) Absolute justice. «لا ظلم الیوم» F) Speed of reckoning. «سریع الحساب»

The Day of Judgment is a very important day. In this Ayah and the previous Ayah, the word «یوم» (Day) has appeared four times: «یوم هم بارزون», «لمن الملك», «الیوم تجزى», «لا ظلم الیوم».

Injustice (zulm) has manifestations: A) Ignoring the efforts of individuals. B) Giving insufficient reward. C) Punishing the criminal more than is His due. None of these will occur on the Day of Judgment, and no one will be wronged. «لا ظلم الیوم»

We read in narrations (Riwāyāt) that the intent of the phrase «لا ظلم الیوم» is that on the Day of Judgment, we will take retribution for the oppressed from the oppressor.(86)

The prolongation of the judicial process in this world is either due to the criminal's denial and concealment of the crime—whereas on the Day of Judgment, the earth, time, body parts, angels, and records of deeds are all witnesses, leaving no room for denial or hiding the crime. Or it is due to the criminal defending himself—whereas on that Day, everyone will confess to their transgressions and have no defense. Or it is due to the summoning of individuals and files—whereas everyone is present and the files are open. Or it is due to the judge's deliberation and review—whereas nothing is hidden from Allah. Or it is due to the lack of capacity in prisons—whereas on that Day, Hell will not be filled. Or it is due to the obstruction caused by the criminal's supporters in court—whereas there will be no intercessor or helper there. Therefore, on the Day of Judgment, Allah attends to the accounts quickly. «ان اللّه سریع الحساب»

86) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, no one is exempt from the reckoning and accounting. «تجزى كل نفس...»

2- Punishment and reward are based on performance (deeds). «تجزى... بما كسبت»

3- Transgressions that carry punishment are those committed with awareness and intent. «كسبت»

4- Usually, singular, centralized powers are oppressive, but Allah, despite being the One, the Irresistible Power (al-Qahhar), does not commit injustice. (His Irresistibility is accompanied by justice). «الواحد القهّار... لا ظلم الیوم»

5- Usually, the crowding and congestion of a court either leads to the slowing down of the judicial process—whereas He is «سریع الحساب» (Swift in Reckoning)—or the rights of the people are not properly fulfilled—whereas «لا ظلم الیوم» (there is no injustice) on that Day.

Ayahs

وَأَنذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْـَٔازِفَةِ إِذِ ٱلْقُلُوبُ لَدَى ٱلْحَنَاجِرِ كَـٰظِمِينَ ۚ مَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ يُطَاعُ (۱۸)
Warn them of the Approaching Day, when the hearts will be at the throats, choking with suppressed agony, [and] the wrongdoers will have no sympathizer, nor any intercessor who might be heard. (18)
يَعْلَمُ خَآئِنَةَ ٱلْأَعْيُنِ وَمَا تُخْفِى ٱلصُّدُورُ (۱۹)
He knows the treachery of the eyes, and what the breasts hide. (19)

Key Points

Azifah means that which is approaching, and the intent of the approaching day is the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). Just as He says elsewhere: «انّهم یرونه بعیداً و نراهُ قریباً» (87)

Kāẓimīn (those who restrain) comes from kaẓm, meaning to close the mouth of a waterskin. A kāẓim is someone who is inwardly distressed but seals his lips and controls his anger and distress.

Looking at a non-mahram (unrelated person of the opposite sex), looking at others contemptuously, and looking to encourage corruption are examples of the treachery of the eyes.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the verse «یعلم خائنة الاعین» (He knows the treachery of the eyes). The Imam said: The intent is furtive glances. «ینظر الى الشى ء و كانّه لا ینظر فذلك خائنة الاعین» (He looks at something as if He is not looking, and that is the treachery of the eyes.) (88)

Imam Kazim عليه السلام said: There is no believer who is not distressed after committing a sin. If he is not distressed, he is not a believer, and there is no intercession for him, and he is an oppressor (ẓālim). Then He recited this verse: «ما للظالمین من حمیم و لا شفیع یطاع» (For the oppressors there will be no intimate friend nor any intercessor who will be obeyed.) (89)

87) Ma'arij, 6 - 7. 88) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 89) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Death and the Resurrection are near; we must prepare ourselves. «یوم الآزفة»

2- On the Day of Resurrection, the criminal neither dies nor finds comfort. «اذ القلوب لدى الحناجر» (Elsewhere we read: «و یأتیه الموت من كل مكان و ما هو بمیّت» (90) Death comes to him from every side, yet he does not die.)

3- On the Day of Resurrection, the existence of criminals is filled with regret and sorrow, but they cannot express it. «كاظمین»

4- The criminal is alone on the Day of Resurrection. (He has neither an intimate friend nor an influential intercessor). «ما للظالمین من حمیم و لا شفیع»

5- Values are based on intentions. A single glance, with different intentions, can be an act of worship or an act of treachery. «خائنة الاعین»

6- Belief that Allah knows the inner reality of everything prevents man from oppression. «ما للظالمین من حمیم... یعلم خائنة الاعین»

7- Allah's knowledge is not limited to the apparent; He also knows the inner reality. «خائنة الاعین و ما تخفى الصّدور» 90) Ibrahim, 17.

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ يَقْضِى بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ لَا يَقْضُونَ بِشَىْءٍ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ (۲٠)
Allah judges with justice, while those whom they invoke besides Him do not judge by anything. Indeed it is Allah who is the All-hearing, the All-seeing. (20)

Messages

1- Judging justly is among the divine attributes. «واللّه یقضى بالحقّ»

2- The prerequisite for judgment is knowledge. «یعلم خائِنة الاعین... و اللّه یقضى بالحقّ»

3- The just Judge must be Seeing and Hearing. «یقضى... هو السمیع البصیر»

4- Deities other than Allah either lack the power of judgment or do not judge justly. «والذّین یدعون من دونه لا یقضون بشى ء»

5- The true Hearer and Seer is only Him. «انّ اللّه هو السمیع البصیر»

Ayah

۞ أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوا۟ هُمْ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَءَاثَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَا كَانَ لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَاقٍ (۲۱)
Have they not travelled through the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were greater than them in might and with respect to the effects [they left] in the land. But then Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no defender against Allah [’s punishment]. (21)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about judging justly (or judging with truth); in this Ayah, He says: an example of judging justly is the divine wrath against the criminals of history.

Messages

1- Travel, journeying, and tourism must be purposeful. «أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا - فَيَنظُرُوا»

2- Experience and knowledge require travel and journeying. «يَسِيرُوا - فَيَنظُرُوا»

3- History is one of the sources of recognition and knowledge. «أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا...»

4- The reason for the disbelievers' opposition to the Prophet of Islam was their infatuation with their own power. Therefore, the Quran says: We destroyed those who were mightier than you. «أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً»

5- Preserving ancient monuments is necessary for the admonition of future generations. «فَيَنظُرُوا - آثَارًا فِي الْأَرْضِ»

6- Let us learn lessons from history. «كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ...»

7- Let us not look at today, but look at the outcome (consequence). «عَاقِبَةُ ...»

8- Let us not boast about our power and monuments. «كَانُوا هُمْ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَآثَارًا»

9- Tyrannical powers are not an obstacle to divine wrath. «أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً... فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ»

10- Power and resources are not the criterion for salvation. «أَشَدَّ قُوَّةً... فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ»

11- Sin is the cause of divine wrath. «أَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ» (The decline of civilizations and the fall of nations is the punishment for their sins and disbelief.)

12- When divine wrath arrives, no defense from any source is effective. «مِن وَاقٍ»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانَت تَّأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَكَفَرُوا۟ فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ قَوِىٌّ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۲۲)
That was because their apostles used to bring them manifest proofs, but they defied [them]. So Allah seized them. Indeed He is all-strong, severe in retribution. (22)

Messages

1- Sending the prophets is a divine tradition (Sunnah) throughout history. «كانت تأتیهم رسلهم...»

2- The prophets went to the people; they did not wait for the people to come to them. «تأتیهم رسلهم»

3- Allah does not hold a nation or an individual accountable until He has completed the argument (Hujjah). «تأتیهم رسلهم... فكفروا... فاخذهم اللّه»

4- All prophets possessed miracles, and there was no ambiguity in their proof and miraculous signs. «تأتیهم رسلهم بالبینات»

5- Disbelief (Kufr) and rebellion are the grounds for the fall and collapse of civilizations. «كفروا... اخذهم اللّه»

6- Suppressing and destroying the disbelievers (Kuffar) is easy for Allah. «انّه قوىّ شدید العقاب»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲۳)
Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority (23)
إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ وَقَـٰرُونَ فَقَالُوا۟ سَـٰحِرٌ كَذَّابٌ (۲٤)
to Pharaoh, Haman and Korah, but they said, ‘A magician and a mendacious liar.’ (24)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوا۟ ٱقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَبْنَآءَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ وَٱسْتَحْيُوا۟ نِسَآءَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا كَيْدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ (۲٥)
So when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, ‘Kill the sons of the faithful who are with him, and spare their women.’ But the stratagems of the faithless only go awry. (25)

Key Points

In the previous verses, we read why they do not travel the earth to observe the fate of the oppressors and take heed. In this verse, as an example, He presents the suppression of Pharaoh (Fir'awn), Haman, and Qarun (Korah). Although the Quran has repeated the story of Moses and Pharaoh many times, in each instance, it shows us a new aspect of the story. In this Surah, reference is made to the beneficial and effective role of a person who was in Pharaoh's court but concealed his faith and played a significant role under the guise of taqiyyah (dissimulation). After the Quran's general statement regarding the coming of the prophets and the disbelief of the people, these verses present a clear example of this in the story of Prophet Moses and his opponents. The words Ayat (signs/verses) and Sultan (authority/proof) have each appeared separately in various contexts, but wherever these two words appear together, they relate to Moses and Pharaoh. This means that Prophet Moses presented both miracles and spoke to Pharaoh with evidence and proof.

Messages

1- Finding a way and infiltrating centers of power is necessary for propagating religion and defending the truth. «و لقد ارسلنا موسى ... الى فرعون»

2- The prophets had two important weapons against the tyrants (taghuts): one was the miracle, «آیاتنا», and the other was clear logic. «سلطان مبین»

3- The basis for people's inclination (to faith) differs; some believe through miracles, and others through logic. The prophets possessed both. «بآیاتنا و سلطان مبین»

4- The forefront of the prophets' mission is the struggle against the leaders of corruption and disbelief; whether in the form of force, governance, and political power, «فرعون», or in the form of planning, preparation, cunning, and cultural power, «هامان», or in the form of capital, wealth, and economic power, «قارون».

5- The content of the prophets' call is the struggle against arrogance (istikbar). «ارسلنا موسى... الى فرعون و...»

6- The prophets had courage and stood against all powers. «الى فرعون و هامان و قارون»

7- Introduce the models of corruption to the people. «فرعون و هامان و قارون»

8- The opponents of the prophets called the miracle magic and the prophets liars. «فرعون و هامان و قارون فقالوا ساحر كذّاب»

9- The essence of religion is truth and reality (Haqq). «جاءهم بالحقّ»

10- Man needs revelation, prophethood, and Divine grace to recognize the path of truth. «الحقّ من عندنا»

11- The opponents of the prophets both assassinate character verbally. «قالوا ساحر كذاب» and practically rise up against them. «اقتلوا...»

12- Killing and martyring the supporters of truth is the method of all arrogant ones (mustakbirun) throughout history. «اقتلوا...»

13- Although the enemy is displeased by people's faith in the prophets, what truly angers him is the practical support of the people for the prophets. «آمنوا معه»

14- The enemy targets youth and women. «اقتلوا ابناء... و استحیوا نساءهم»

15- The disbelievers plot, but they do not achieve their goal, and their scheme is foiled. «و ما كيد الكافرين الا فى ضلال»

16- Allah consoles the Prophet and the believers by recounting the neutralization of the Pharaonic conspiracies. «و ما كيد الكافرين الا فى ضلال»

Ayah

وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ذَرُونِىٓ أَقْتُلْ مُوسَىٰ وَلْيَدْعُ رَبَّهُۥٓ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُبَدِّلَ دِينَكُمْ أَوْ أَن يُظْهِرَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْفَسَادَ (۲٦)
And Pharaoh said, ‘Let me slay Moses, and let him invoke his Lord. Indeed I fear that he will change your religion, or bring forth corruption in our land.’ (26)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about killing the young men of the Children of Israel. It seems this action was not sufficient for Pharaoh, and the main source of danger in his view was Moses himself. Therefore, this Ayah raises the issue of killing Moses. Killing Moses would have difficult repercussions in public opinion, so Pharaoh consulted and deliberated with his advisors and courtiers. Pharaoh's statement, "ذرونى" (Leave me alone), indicates that his advisors were reluctant to agree to the killing of Moses.

Messages

1- The logic of tyrants is to kill the leaders of truth. «قال فرعون ذرونى اقتل موسى»

2- The method of the arrogant is either denial «فقالوا ساحر كذاب», or threat «ذرونى اقتل موسى», or humiliation. «و لیدع ربّه»

3- Pharaoh's rule was a tyrannical, autocratic, and lawless government. «اقتل موسى»

4- The criminal justifies his crime. (Pharaoh cites the fear of the people's religion being changed as the reason for killing him.) «اقتل... انى اخاف»

5- Tyrants present themselves as friends of the people. «انى اخاف ان یبدل دینكم»

6- Wherever tyrants see their rule in danger, they introduce themselves as supporters of the people, advocates of religion, and reformers. «انى اخاف ان یبدل دینكم او ان یظهر... الفساد»

7- Religion, in the Pharaonic reading, means submission to the tyrant. «یبدل دینكم»

8- Establishing peace and security is the pretext used by tyrants to suppress religious and reform movements. «اقتل موسى... اخاف ان یبدل دینكم او ان یظهر... الفساد»

9- Tyrants introduce the reformers of history as corruptors. «یظهر فى الارض الفساد»

10- Tyrants consider their survival dependent on maintaining the status quo and oppose every reform movement. «انى اخاف ان یبدل دینكم»

11- Prophets intervened in political and social matters, and thus they were persecuted and harmed by the tyrants. «یبدل دینكم او یظهر فى الارض الفساد»

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنِّى عُذْتُ بِرَبِّى وَرَبِّكُم مِّن كُلِّ مُتَكَبِّرٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۲۷)
Moses said, ‘I seek the protection of my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.’ (27)

Key Points

By saying, «عُذت بربّى و ربّكم» (I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord), Moses, while declaring his own refuge in Allah, told the people: Pharaoh is not your god. The proverb, "Do not break the salt shaker where you have eaten salt" (meaning: do not betray your benefactor), is not always correct, because Moses was at Pharaoh's table for years, but since Pharaoh did not accept the truth, he was ultimately afflicted by Divine wrath.

Messages

1- In the face of enemy threats, we must seek refuge in Allah. «انّى عُذتُ بربّى»

2- We must seek refuge in the One in Whose hand are the affairs of all of us, and under Whose Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) we all exist. «بربّى»

3- The person of Pharaoh is not important; what is important is the Pharaonic characteristic, which is arrogance (takabbur), and one must seek refuge in Allah from it. «كل متكبّر»

4- The Prophets were brave. In the face of the threat of murder, Moses told Pharaoh: You are arrogant. «متكبّر»

5- Lack of faith in accountability (Hisāb wa Kitāb) makes a person arrogant. «متكبّر لا یؤمن»

Ayah

وَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مُّؤْمِنٌ مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إِيمَـٰنَهُۥٓ أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلًا أَن يَقُولَ رَبِّىَ ٱللَّهُ وَقَدْ جَآءَكُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ كَـٰذِبًا فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُۥ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ صَادِقًا يُصِبْكُم بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى يَعِدُكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ كَذَّابٌ (۲۸)
Said a man of faith from Pharaoh’s clan, who concealed his faith, ‘Will you kill a man for saying, ‘‘My Lord is Allah,’’ while he has already brought you manifest proofs from your Lord? Should he be lying, his falsehood will be to his own detriment; but if he is truthful, there shall visit you some of what he promises you. Indeed Allah does not guide someone who is a transgressor and liar. (28)

Key Points

The story of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh (Mu'min Āl Fir'awn) is mentioned only in this Surah. He was a relative of Pharaoh who had secretly believed in Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), but he concealed his faith so that he could speak in favor of Moses or perform a service for him when the time was right. This person is different from Pharaoh's wife and different from the man who quickly came to Moses from the far end of the city and informed him of the plot to arrest him. Fakhr Razi and Maraghi state in their respective Tafsirs (commentaries) that he was one of Pharaoh's courtiers and his chief of police. Based on a Hadith (narration) cited by Shaykh Saduq in Amali and Fakhr Razi in his Tafsir, the Siddiqūn (Truthful Ones) are three: Habib al-Najjar (the Believer of Āl Yāsīn), the Believer from the family of Pharaoh, and Ali ibn Abi Talib (عليه السلام), who is the most excellent among them. The Believer from the family of Pharaoh, instead of using great titles for Moses, referred to him as an unknown man, saying: «رجلاً» (a man), so that Pharaoh would not realize his faith. Isrāf (extravagance/transgression) refers to any kind of exceeding the limit, whether it is transgression in consumption or transgression in conduct and morals. Anyone who ignores the proofs and miracles of the Prophets is a musrif (transgressor). In Islamic texts, Abu Talib, the father of Imam Ali (عليهما السلام), has been likened to the Believer from the family of Pharaoh regarding the manner of his faith.(91)

91) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- One manifestation of Divine aid, which results from seeking refuge in Him, is the emergence of supporters from among the opponents. «عذت بربّى... قال رجلٌ»

2- Keep the memory of historical debates and arguments alive. (The Quran narrates the method of argumentation used by the Believer from the family of Pharaoh). «و قال رجل...»

3- When recounting history, focus on the essential elements, not names and titles. «رجل مؤمن»

4- Working within a system of oppression is not an obstacle if it serves a more important mission. «رجل مؤمن من آل فرعون»

5- A corrupt system, organization, society, or environment does not necessitate a person committing a crime. (Believing individuals can be found even within the Pharaonic system.) «و قال رجل مؤمن من آل فرعون»

6- Sometimes taqiyyah (dissimulation) and concealing one's belief are necessary for carrying out important tasks and achieving higher goals. «یكتم ایمانه»

7- The blessings of Nahy 'anil-Munkar (forbidding evil) extend so far that it saves a Prophet from being killed, and that Prophet, in turn, saves a society. «أتقتلون رجلاً»

8- A believer is not indifferent; he supports the oppressed and cries out against the oppressor. «أتقتلون رجلاً»

9- Awaken dormant consciences through questioning. «أتقتلون...»

10- In a tyrannical system, even proclaiming the name of Allah is a crime. «أتقتلون رجلاً ان یقول ربّى اللّه»

11- The believer defends with logic. «أتقتلون... جاءكم بالبینات»

12- In the method of propagation, set aside fanaticism. (The Believer from the family of Pharaoh said: Moses is either truthful or a liar; if he is truthful, what He promises will come to pass, and if he is a liar, the burden is upon himself.) «ان یك كاذبا... و ان یك صادقا»

13- The harm of a lie returns to the liar himself. «ان یك كاذبا فعلیه كذبه»

14- Transgression (isrāf) and denial (takdhīb) are two obstacles to guidance. «لا یهدى هو مسرف كذاب»

Ayah

يَـٰقَوْمِ لَكُمُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ٱلْيَوْمَ ظَـٰهِرِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَمَن يَنصُرُنَا مِنۢ بَأْسِ ٱللَّهِ إِن جَآءَنَا ۚ قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ مَآ أُرِيكُمْ إِلَّا مَآ أَرَىٰ وَمَآ أَهْدِيكُمْ إِلَّا سَبِيلَ ٱلرَّشَادِ (۲۹)
O my people! Today sovereignty belongs to you, and you are dominant in the land. But who will save us from Allah’s punishment should it overtake us?’ Pharaoh said, ‘I just point out to you what I see [to be advisable for you], and I guide you only to the way of rectitude.’ (29)

Messages

1- The believer is compassionate toward the people. (The phrase «یا قوم» [O my people] is repeated six times in verses 29 to 41.)

2- In the method of propagation, we should utilize the element of emotion. «یا قوم»

3- The sign of a believer, even under a regime of disbelief (kufr), is effort and presence in the field, not isolation and withdrawal. «یكتم ایمانه... یا قوم...»

4- We should not rely on our own power simply because it is in our hands today. «لكم الملك الیوم»

5- In the Pharaonic system, faith is hidden, and transgression (tyranny) is manifest. «یكتم ایمانه - ظاهرین فى الارض»

6- A warning is necessary for individuals intoxicated by power. «فمن ینصرنا من بأس اللّه»

7- Disregard for the invitation of the prophets leads to Divine wrath. «فمن ینصرنا من بأس اللّه اِن جاءنا»

8- When issuing a warning, stating the probability of danger is sufficient. «بأس اللّه اِن جاءنا»

9- In the method of propagation and warning people, consider yourselves also exposed to danger. «جاءنا» instead of «جاءكم».

10- Tyrants (Taghuts) do not pay attention to warnings. «یا قوم... قال فرعون ما اُریكم...»

11- The repeated use of the first-person singular ("I") is a sign of a tyrant. «اهدیكم، اُریكم، ما اَرى»

12- Tyrants grant the right to express an opinion only to themselves, not to anyone else. «ما اُریكم الا ما اَرى»

13- Tyrants claim to guide people to the correct path and the welfare of society. «ما اهدیكم الا سبیل الرشاد»

14- Man instinctively desires happiness, growth, and guidance, and tyrants exploit this innate motivation. «الا سبیل الرشاد»

15- Tyrants use religion against religion. (Pharaoh used the word «رَشاد» [rectitude], which carries ideological weight.)

Ayahs

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنَ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُم مِّثْلَ يَوْمِ ٱلْأَحْزَابِ (۳٠)
He who had faith said, ‘O my people! Indeed I fear for you [a day] like the day of the [heathen] factions; (30)
مِثْلَ دَأْبِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۚ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْمًا لِّلْعِبَادِ (۳۱)
like the case of the people of Noah, of Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them, and Allah does not desire any wrong for [His] servants. (31)

Key Points

The nations destroyed after the people of Noah, 'Ad, and Thamud, referred to in Ayah 31, are the people of Lot and the dwellers of Al-Aykah (the people of Shu'ayb), who are mentioned in Ayah 13 of Surah Sad. In contrast to Pharaoh, who deceitfully said: «انّى اخاف علیكم» (I fear for you), the Believer from the family of Pharaoh also said: «انّى اخاف علیكم» (I fear for you), meaning, I fear for your ultimate fate.

Messages

  1. Do not cease preaching and guiding due to the words of opponents. «قال فرعون... قال الذّى آمن»
  2. Being alone is not an excuse for abandoning enjoining good and forbidding evil. «قال الذّى آمن»
  3. Preaching is among the necessities of faith. «قال الذّى آمن یا قوم»
  4. The preacher must possess passion (or deep concern). «یا قوم»
  5. The preacher must be aware of history. «مثل یوم الاحزاب»
  6. In the method of preaching, sometimes one should speak generally, and sometimes one should state specific examples and instances. «مثل یوم الاحزاب مثل دأب قوم نوح و...»
  7. The great danger is when the denial of truth becomes a habit and characteristic in a person. «دأب»
  8. Allah's wrath is not exclusive to the Day of Judgment; nations have been punished in this very world. «مثل دأب قوم نوح و عاد و ثمود»
  9. Among Allah's traditions (Sunan) is the destruction of tyrants. «قوم نوح و عاد و ثمود و الذّین من بعدهم»
  10. In the method of preaching, wherever punishment is mentioned, state the reason for the punishment so that you are not accused of harshness. «نوح و عاد و ثمود... و ما اللّه یرید ظلماً للعباد» Their destruction was based on justice.
  11. Allah's wrath is due to our own actions and is based on justice and wisdom. «و ما اللّه یُرید ظلماً للعباد»
  12. Allah does not even intend injustice towards His servants. «ما اللّه یُرید ظلماً للعباد»
  13. The worst injustice is when a person oppresses those under his authority. «ظلماً للعباد»

Ayahs

وَيَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلتَّنَادِ (۳۲)
O my people! I fear for you a day of mutual distress calls, (32)
يَوْمَ تُوَلُّونَ مُدْبِرِينَ مَا لَكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ عَاصِمٍ ۗ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ (۳۳)
a day when you will turn back [to flee], not having anyone to protect you from Allah, and whomever Allah leads astray has no guide. (33)

Key Points

The term tanād (تناد) is derived from nidā’ (نداء), meaning to call one another. On the Day of Resurrection, wails and cries will be loud. The believer will cry out, "Come and read my record: «هاؤم اقْرءوا كتابیه»"(92). The disbeliever, crying out, will express his regret with the words «یا لیتنى»(93) and «یا ویلتى »(94). The people of Paradise will call out to the people of Hell, asking why they entered Hell: «ما سلككم فى سقر»(95). And the people of Hell will seek help from the people of Paradise, asking them to give them some of what Allah has provided for them. In any case, calls will be loud from every direction. The meaning of Allah causing the criminals to go astray is that He abandons them to their own devices, not that He diverts them from the straight path and misguides them. This is exactly like a child who incurs the father's wrath and is deprived of his guidance, which then leads to his misery and destruction.

92) Al-Haaqqah, 19. 93) An-Naba’, 40. 94) Al-Furqan, 28. 95) Al-Muddaththir, 42.

Messages

1- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), we should not expect to achieve results with the first reminder; rather, repetition is necessary. («یا قوم» was repeated.)

2- In the method of propagation, we should not forget emotions (or appeal to feelings). «یا قوم»

3- In the method of propagation, we should make greater use of the tool of warning (Inzār). («انى اخاف» was repeated)

4- In the method of propagation, we should remind people both of Allah's wrath in this world. «قوم نوح و عاد...» and His divine wrath on the Day of Resurrection. «مالكم من اللّه من عاصم»

5- Fleeing on the Day of Resurrection will have no effect. «تولّون... مالكم من اللّه من عاصم»

6- Guidance (Hidāyah) and misguidance (Ḍalālah) are in the hands of Allah, although the prerequisites for them are in the hands of man. «من یضل اللّه فما له من هاد»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جَآءَكُمْ يُوسُفُ مِن قَبْلُ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا زِلْتُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّمَّا جَآءَكُم بِهِۦ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا هَلَكَ قُلْتُمْ لَن يَبْعَثَ ٱللَّهُ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رَسُولًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ مُّرْتَابٌ (۳٤)
Certainly Joseph brought you manifest proofs earlier, but you continued to remain in doubt concerning what he had brought you. When he died, you said, ‘‘Allah will never send any apostle after him.’’ That is how Allah leads astray those who are unrestrained and skeptical. (34)

Key Points

The Believer from the family of Pharaoh, in order to guide and awaken the people, first presented the history of their predecessors and said: Beware that the calamity which befell the people of Noah, 'Ad, and Thamud does not also reach you. Then he brought up the past record of the Children of Israel, reminding them that they did such and such regarding Joseph. Any kind of transgression beyond the limit is called isrāf (extravagance/excess), and the intent of musrif (the extravagant one) in this Ayah is someone who insists and persists in opposing the truth. (96)

96) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- The behavior of ancestors influences the inclinations or obstinacy of their descendants. The Believer from the family of Pharaoh said: If you do not believe in Moses today, you have not gone far astray; you did not believe in Joseph either. «جاءكم يوسف من قبل»

2- Joseph had many clear proofs (miracles). «بالبينات»

3- Doubt is valuable if it becomes a prelude to research and movement, but it is a calamity if it causes stagnation and suspicion. «فما زلتم في شك»

4- Doubt is a natural state, but unwarranted skepticism is disliked. «فمازلتم في شك مما جاءكم به»

5- Death is for everyone, even the Prophets. «حتى إذا هلك»

6- The belief in the finality of prophethood after Joseph caused some of the Children of Israel not to accept the mission of Prophet Moses. «قلتم لن يبعث اللّه من بعده رسولا»

7- Our satisfaction with the crime of others makes us partners in that crime. (Joseph lived four hundred years before Moses, but because the later generations were satisfied with the disbelief of their ancestors, Allah addressed them). (97) «قلتم لن يبعث اللّه»

8- The finality of the Prophet (Prophethood) rests with Allah, not the desire of the people. «قلتم لن يبعث اللّه من بعده رسولا»

9- Allah does not leave people without proof and guidance. (The Ayah criticizes those who say without evidence: «لن يبعث اللّه... رسولا»).

10- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to leave those who persist in opposing the truth to their own devices. «كذلك يُضِلّ اللّه من هو مسرف مرتاب»

11- Whoever does not accept the path of the Prophets and does not move forward has wasted the talents, merits, and favors that he was entitled to receive. «مسرف مرتاب»

12- The downfall of man is gradual: in one stage he is skeptical, «مسرف مرتاب», but in another stage, he is a denier. «مسرف كذّاب» (98)

13- The grace (Fayḍ) of Allah is universal; it is these individuals who deprive themselves due to their bad attitudes and actions. «يُضلّ اللّه من هو مسرف مرتاب»

97) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan.

98) Ghafir, 28.

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُجَـٰدِلُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطَـٰنٍ أَتَىٰهُمْ ۖ كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَعِندَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ قَلْبِ مُتَكَبِّرٍ جَبَّارٍ (۳٥)
Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them—[that is] greatly outrageous to Allah and to those who have faith. That is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.’ (35)

Key Points

The word Sultān (سلطان) means proof and evidence (hujjah and burhān), and Maqt (مَقت) means intense anger and wrath. This Ayah is an interpretation of the verses which state that Allah seals hearts, because here it states that Allah's seal is placed upon the hearts of the arrogant tyrants. «یطبع اللّه...»

Messages

1- Argumentation stemming from ignorance, thoughtlessness, and lack of evidence leads to doubt, skepticism, and deprivation from Divine guidance. «یضل اللّه من هو مسرف مرتاب الذّین یجادلون»

2- Argumentation also has its own specific method and ethics, and baseless argumentation is rejected. «یجادلون فى ایات اللّه بغیر سلطان...»

3- The Prophets possess clear proofs, «جاءكم بالبینات», but the opponents lack evidence. «بغیر سلطان»

4- Proof and evidence lead to a kind of dominance and authority over others. «بغیر سلطان»

5- Baseless disputants are deprived of popularity (love) in the sight of Allah and the believers. «مقتاً عنداللّه و عند الذّین آمنوا»

6- Allah's wrath is governed by law (it is systematic). «كذلك»

7- Undue arrogance deprives a person from receiving Divine favors. «یطبع اللّه على كل قلب متكبّر جبّار»

8- In a system where the ruler is the arrogant and tyrannical Pharaoh, words must be used in guiding the people that allow them to find the practical example (of the warning). «یطبع اللّه على كل قلب متكبّر جبّار»

9- The reason for denying the prophethood of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) (which was mentioned in the previous Ayah) was the spirit of arrogance. «متكبّر جبّار»

10- Believers must take a stand against illogical disputants and arguers, dissociate themselves from them, and declare their anger. «یجادلون... بغیر سلطان اتاهم كبر مقتاً... عند الذّین آمنوا»

Ayahs

وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَـٰهَـٰمَـٰنُ ٱبْنِ لِى صَرْحًا لَّعَلِّىٓ أَبْلُغُ ٱلْأَسْبَـٰبَ (۳٦)
Pharaoh said, ‘O Haman! Build me a tower so that I may reach the routes (36)
أَسْبَـٰبَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ فَأَطَّلِعَ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِ مُوسَىٰ وَإِنِّى لَأَظُنُّهُۥ كَـٰذِبًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِفِرْعَوْنَ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ وَصُدَّ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۚ وَمَا كَيْدُ فِرْعَوْنَ إِلَّا فِى تَبَابٍ (۳۷)
—the routes of the heavens—and take a look at the God of Moses, and indeed I consider him a liar.’ The evil of his conduct was thus presented as decorous to Pharaoh, and he was kept from the way [of Allah]. Pharaoh’s stratagems only led him into ruin. (37)

Key Points

Ṣarḥ (صَرح) means a tall structure visible from afar, and taṣrīḥ (تصریح) refers to speech that is clear. Tabāb (تباب) means continuity in loss, just as "تَبَّتْ يَدَا أَبِي لَهَبٍ" (May the hands of Abu Lahab perish) means prolonged destruction and absolute annihilation for Abu Lahab. The admonitions of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh proved useful. Pharaoh, who chanted the slogan "اُقْتُلْ مُوسَىٰ" (Kill Moses) and said, "I will kill Moses, and no one should have an opinion other than mine," ("مَا أُرِيكُمْ إِلَّا مَا أَرَىٰ"), after the efforts of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh, instead of killing Moses, started thinking about building a tower to find the God of Moses.

Messages

1- As soon as tyrants lose in the field of logic, they resort to sensational actions. "ابْنِ لِي صَرْحًا..." (Build for me a tower...) (The goal of building the tower and observatory was sensationalism, because the end of the Ayah states: "كَيْدُ فِرْعَوْنَ" [Pharaoh's plot]).

2- Materialists perceive everything as physical. (Pharaoh imagined God to be in the heavens and the path to knowing God only through the senses and sight). "فَأَطَّلِعَ إِلَىٰ إِلَٰهِ مُوسَىٰ" (That I may look upon the God of Moses).

3- The method of tyrants is deception and display of power. "ابْنِ لِي صَرْحًا" (Build for me a tower).

4- Preaching and invitation are not without effect. (Pharaoh initially decided to kill Moses. "ذَرُونِي أَقْتُلْ مُوسَىٰ" [Leave me to kill Moses], but due to the words of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh, he moved from certainty to doubt. "لَأَظُنُّهُ كَاذِبًا" [I surely think him a liar]).

5- The disbeliever assumes everyone follows his own creed. (Since Pharaoh himself made a false claim, saying: "أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلَىٰ" [I am your Lord, the Most High], he also considered Moses a liar). "لَأَظُنُّهُ كَاذِبًا".

6- One of Satan's methods is the beautification of evil deeds for man. "زُيِّنَ لِفِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ" (The evil of his deeds was made fair-seeming to Pharaoh).

7- Whoever sees his own evil as beautiful becomes misguided in evaluation, does not think about changing his method, and is deprived of the path of truth. "زُيِّنَ... وَصُدَّ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ" (Made fair-seeming... and averted from the path).

8- Do not worry; the plots of the tyrants have no lasting consequence and lead only to their detriment. "إِلَّا فِي تَبَابٍ" (Except in ruin).

Ayahs

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱتَّبِعُونِ أَهْدِكُمْ سَبِيلَ ٱلرَّشَادِ (۳۸)
And he who had faith said, ‘O my people! Follow me; I will guide you to the way of rectitude. (38)
يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّمَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا مَتَـٰعٌ وَإِنَّ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ هِىَ دَارُ ٱلْقَرَارِ (۳۹)
O my people! This life of the world is only a [passing] enjoyment, and indeed the Hereafter is the abiding home. (39)

Messages

1- Sometimes one must break taqiyyah (dissimulation) and express one's belief. (The Believer from the family of Pharaoh, who had hidden his faith for a time, broke taqiyyah, took a stand against Pharaoh, and told the people: Follow me.) «و قال الذّى آمن یا قوم اتبعون»

2- The duty of the believer is to invite others. «و قال الذّى آمن یا قوم اتبعون»

3- When inviting people, sometimes one must stand up single-handedly and not wait for others. «قال الذّى آمن»

4- Propagating the religion in an oppressive tyrannical atmosphere is so valuable that Allah recounts it in the Quran and honors its memory. «و قال الذّى امن...»

5- Following the word of the believer is following God and the Messenger. «اتبعونى»

6- In the method of propagation, disarm the enemy and appropriate his beautiful slogans. (In Ayah 29, we read that Pharaoh said: «ما اهدیكم الا سبیل الرشاد» I only guide you to the path of rectitude. In this Ayah, we read that the Believer from the family of Pharaoh also says: «اهدكم سبیل الرشاد» (Yes, reaching rectitude (rushd) and felicity is the aspiration of all human beings, which the arrogant misuse, and believers must utilize this innate need correctly.) «اتبعونى اهدكم سبیل الرشاد»

7- Rectitude (rushd) lies in distinguishing the status of this world from the Hereafter. «سبیل الرشاد یا قوم انّما...»

8- Belief in the transience of this world and the permanence of the Resurrection is the basis for felicity and rectitude. «سبیل الرشاد... الحیاة الدنیا متاع»

9- The world is nothing but a means for temporary gratification. «متاع» (This word refers to something from which little benefit is derived.)

10- By comparing the value of this world and the Hereafter, we better understand the realities. «متاع... دارالقرار»

11- The splendor of this world is fleeting; let us think about the eternal world. «متاع - دارالقرار»

12- In the method of propagation, we must pay attention to the natural and innate desires of the people. (Eternal life is a natural and innate desire of man, and the Believer from the family of Pharaoh introduces the attainment of this desire as dependent upon following the path of truth.) «یا قوم... الدنیا متاع و انّ الآخرة هى دار القرار»

Ayah

مَنْ عَمِلَ سَيِّئَةً فَلَا يُجْزَىٰٓ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ يُرْزَقُونَ فِيهَا بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (٤٠)
Whoever commits a misdeed shall not be requited except with its like, but whoever acts righteously, whether male or female, should he be faithful—such shall enter paradise, and they will be provided therein without any reckoning. (40)

Messages

1- Punishment and reward are based on action (not imagination or wishes). «مَن عمل»

2- Allah is Just, and His punishment is not more than the extent of the crime. «مثلها»

3- Man has been created with free will. «مَن عمل سيئة... مَن عمل صالحاً»

4- Good and bad deeds are not without consequence, whether they are great or small. «سيئةً - صالحاً»

5- Comparison between good and bad deeds, and reward and punishment, is the best method of understanding. «عمل سيئة... عمل صالحا»

6- Good deeds are not related to a person's gender (being male or female). «مَن عمل صالحاً من ذكر او انثى»

7- Encouragement is necessary to create motivation for worthy deeds. «مَن عمل صالحاً... فاولئك یدخلون الجنة»

8- Men and women are equal in traversing the path of perfection and receiving Divine favors. «من ذكر او انثى»

9- Faith and action are effective when together, and neither of them alone brings salvation. «عمل - و هو مؤمن»

10- Faith is a condition for the acceptance of deeds. «عمل... و هو مؤمن»

11- Entering Paradise requires faith. «و هو مؤمن فاولئك یدخلون الجنّة»

12- The reward from Allah is without measure. (Allah's Mercy precedes His Wrath). «بغیر حساب» (The meaning of «بغیر حساب» is the abundance of the reward, not that it is without reckoning or order.)

Ayahs

۞ وَيَـٰقَوْمِ مَا لِىٓ أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى ٱلنَّجَوٰةِ وَتَدْعُونَنِىٓ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ (٤۱)
O my people! [Think,] what makes me invite you to deliverance, while you invite me toward the Fire? (41)
تَدْعُونَنِى لِأَكْفُرَ بِٱللَّهِ وَأُشْرِكَ بِهِۦ مَا لَيْسَ لِى بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ وَأَنَا۠ أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرِ (٤۲)
You invite me to defy Allah and to ascribe to Him partners of which I have no knowledge, while I call you to the All-mighty, the All-forgiver. (42)

Key Points

The Believer from the family of Pharaoh gradually lifts the veil of taqiyyah (dissimulation) and makes the call towards Allah clearer moment by moment. From Ayah 29 until this point, the phrase "يا قوم" (O my people) has appeared several times, which indicates the fervor and insistence of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh to guide the people, even under the Pharaonic regime. In these two verses, the phrase "أَدْعُوكُمْ" (I call you) appears twice, and the phrase "تَدْعُونَنِي" (you call me) appears twice, all using the imperfect tense (present/future tense). This is a sign of the continuity and persistence of the call from the two fronts: truth and falsehood.

Messages

1- If there is fervor, faith, and steadfastness, a person can rise up alone against a multitude of deviants and cry out. "أَدْعُوكُمْ" (I call you).

2- Conflict and opposition between truth and falsehood have always existed. "أَدْعُوكُمْ - تَدْعُونَنِي" (I call you - you call me).

3- The multitude of callers to falsehood should not affect your solitary call. ("تَدْعُونَنِي" is plural, and "أَدْعُوكُمْ" is singular).

4- Corrupt regimes are intent on deviating the believers. "تَدْعُونَنِي" (you call me).

5- The path of truth leads to salvation, and the path of falsehood leads to the Fire (Hell). "إِلَى النَّجَاةِ - إِلَى النَّارِ" (to salvation - to the Fire).

6- In calling to the truth, do not only approach ordinary people, but also approach the heralds of disbelief and polytheism. "أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى النَّجَاةِ وَتَدْعُونَنِي لِأَكْفُرَ بِاللَّهِ وَأُشْرِكَ" (I call you to salvation, and you call me to disbelieve in Allah and associate partners).

7- The polytheist lacks logic and proof. "أُشْرِكَ بِهِ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ" (that I associate with Him that for which I have no knowledge).

8- Use the best expressions to attract deviants. (The fact that he said: What is wrong with me, "مَالِي" (What is wrong with me), and did not say: "مَالَكُمْ" (What is wrong with you), as well as the use of the terms salvation (najāt), the All-Mighty (Azīz), and the All-Forgiving (Ghaffār), is intended to attract deviants toward the path of growth and felicity).

9- Repentance and return from disbelief and polytheism are accepted. "أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى الْعَزِيزِ الْغَفَّارِ" (I call you to the All-Mighty, the All-Forgiving).

10- Allah's forgiveness is not due to weakness or inability, but He is forgiving despite His power. "الْعَزِيزِ الْغَفَّارِ" (The All-Mighty, the All-Forgiving).

Ayah

لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّمَا تَدْعُونَنِىٓ إِلَيْهِ لَيْسَ لَهُۥ دَعْوَةٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَلَا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَنَّ مَرَدَّنَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَأَنَّ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ هُمْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ (٤۳)
Undoubtedly, that to which you invite me has no invitation in the world nor in the Hereafter, and indeed our return will be to Allah, and indeed it is the transgressors who will be inmates of the Fire. (43)

Key Points

Jaram (جَرَم) means to cut off or to pick fruit, and lā jarama (لا جرم) means: that which cannot be cut off, continuous, it is always thus, and there is no alternative. This verse is also a continuation of the guiding words of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh. The phrase laysa lahu da‘wah (لیس له دعوة) can be interpreted in several ways: a) Idols and tyrants do not have the power to answer any prayer (du'a). b) Idols and tyrants do not have a message of invitation (da'wah) for the guidance or salvation of humanity. c) Idols and tyrants are not worthy of our invitation (or calling upon them).

Messages

1- In matters of belief, faith and decisiveness are necessary. (lā jarama)

2- In enjoining good and forbidding evil, we must speak with evidence. (Idols cannot invite people to themselves). (laysa lahu da‘wah)

3- Extravagance (isrāf) is not limited to wealth; rather, wasting human talents and energies by adhering to idols that are nothing more than inanimate objects is also extravagance. (inna al-musrifīna hum aṣḥābu an-nār)

Ayahs

فَسَتَذْكُرُونَ مَآ أَقُولُ لَكُمْ ۚ وَأُفَوِّضُ أَمْرِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَصِيرٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ (٤٤)
Soon you will remember what I tell you, and I entrust my affair to Allah. Indeed Allah watches His servants.’ (44)
فَوَقَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا۟ ۖ وَحَاقَ بِـَٔالِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوٓءُ ٱلْعَذَابِ (٤٥)
Then Allah saved him from their evil schemes, while a terrible punishment besieged Pharaoh’s clan: (45)

Key Points

“Tafwidh” (entrustment) is entrusting affairs to Allah, and this state is higher than Tawakkul (reliance). Because in agency (wikalah), the principal (muwakkil) can supervise the agent’s (wakil) work, but in Tafwidh, we entrust all affairs to Allah. (99) Of course, this Tafwidh and entrusting affairs to Allah, which was the motto of the Believer from the family of Pharaoh, came after exerting all his effort to save Moses from murder, and after awakening, warning, and propagating (the message). It is this kind of Tafwidh that brings various forms of Divine support. «فوقاه اللّه» (So Allah protected him). It is understood from these two verses that the family of Pharaoh was threatening this believer and devising schemes against him, but Allah protected him from all plots and dangers. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: The Pharaohites killed the Believer from the family of Pharaoh, and the meaning of «وقاه اللّه» is that Allah preserved his religion. (100)

99) Tafsir Namuneh. 100) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The preacher must be hopeful and see a bright future. «فستذكرون...»

2- In propagation, sometimes one must issue a threat. «فستذكرون ما اقول لكم» (In the future, you will be jolted out of heedlessness by Divine wrath).

3- The preacher must be certain of his goals. «فستذكرون ما اقول لكم»

4- Against threats and plots «مكروا» (they plotted), one must seek refuge in Allah. «اُفوّض امرى الى اللّه» (I entrust my affair to Allah).

5- The preacher must have a spiritual support. «اُفوّض امرى الى اللّه»

6- We should entrust affairs to One who has complete awareness of our condition. «بصیر بالعباد» (He is All-Seeing of the servants).

7- Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) and entrusting affairs (Tafwidh) to Him brings a swift Divine response. «فوقاه اللّه»

8- If Allah wills, He protects and aids a single believer amidst a corrupt and scheming regime. «فوقاه اللّه»

9- By the grace of Allah, both the lives and faith of believers are protected from plots, and the plotters themselves are destroyed. «فوقاه اللّه... و حاق بآل فرعون»

10- Association and companionship with oppressors make a person share in their punishment. «و حاق بآل فرعون»

11- Divine punishments have degrees. «سوء العذاب» (the worst of the punishment).

Ayah

ٱلنَّارُ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْهَا غُدُوًّا وَعَشِيًّا ۖ وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ أَدْخِلُوٓا۟ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ أَشَدَّ ٱلْعَذَابِ (٤٦)
the Fire, to which they are exposed morning and evening. On the day when the Hour sets in, Pharaoh’s clan will enter the severest punishment. (46)

Key Points

The criminals, after death and entering the world of Barzakh (the intermediate realm), are presented to the Fire—which is their future abode—every morning and evening, and they experience intense dread. This state continues until the Day of Resurrection, when they officially enter Hell (Dūzakh). (101)

(101) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn and Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages
