1- Between death and the Day of Resurrection, there is a period during which the sinner sees his abode in the Fire. «النار یعرضون علیها...»

2- The criminal is tormented in Barzakh every morning and evening. «النار یعرضون علیها...»

3- Divine punishments are varied. (In the previous Ayah, it was «سوء العذاب» [the worst of the punishment], and in this Ayah, it is «اشدّ العذاب» [the severest of the punishment]).

Ayah

وَإِذْ يَتَحَآجُّونَ فِى ٱلنَّارِ فَيَقُولُ ٱلضُّعَفَـٰٓؤُا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًا فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّغْنُونَ عَنَّا نَصِيبًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ (٤۷)
When they argue in the Fire, the oppressed will say to the oppressors, ‘We used to follow you; will you avail us against any portion of the Fire?’ (47)

Key Points

The word ihtijaj (argumentation/disputation), wherever it appears in the Quran, is used by misguided individuals. «و اذ یتحاجّون فى النّار» In the preceding verses, the discussion was about Allah's wrath upon the people of Pharaoh. These verses describe the conversation between the arrogant ones (the Pharaohs of history) and their flattering entourages and helpers in Hell. Indeed, feelings of inferiority and low self-esteem compel some individuals to gather around tyrants (taghuts), but this is unacceptable according to reason, logic, and revelation. This is similar to how life's difficulties sometimes compel people toward wrongdoing, whereas problems should be solved through patience (sabr), asceticism (zuhd), and effort, not by resorting to transgression.

Messages

1- Remembering the conversations of the inhabitants of Hell is a factor for alertness and awakening. «اذ»

2- The hostility and disputation among the inhabitants of Hell are continuous. «یتحاجّون»

3- The fire of Hell, despite all its severity and pain, does not obliterate man's power of perception and understanding. «یتحاجّون»

4- The inhabitants of Hell engage in arguments and pleas for help with one another. «یتحاجّون فى النار»

5- Worldly difficulties are not a justification for submission to the tyrant (taghut); rather, this submission will drag man into Hell. «فى النار فیقول الضعفاء...»

6- In Hell, the criminals recognize one another, remember the world, and possess the ability to speak. «یتحاجّون - فیقول»

7- The ultimate consequence of inappropriate imitation and following is Hell. «انّا كنّا لكم تبعاً»

8- The danger lies where man is a follower of falsehood with his entire being. «تبع» (Meaning: We were wholly followers, in contrast to tabi')

9- Following falsehood creates problems; we must pay attention to whom we are following. «انا كنا لكم تبعاً»

10- Due to helplessness on the Day of Judgment, the criminal seeks refuge with another criminal. «فهل انتم مغنون عنّا»

11- Out of desperation, the criminal is content with a reduction in a portion of the punishment. «نصیباً من النار»

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُلٌّ فِيهَآ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَدْ حَكَمَ بَيْنَ ٱلْعِبَادِ (٤۸)
The oppressors will say, ‘We are all in it [together]; Allah has judged between [His] servants.’ (48)
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ فِى ٱلنَّارِ لِخَزَنَةِ جَهَنَّمَ ٱدْعُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ يُخَفِّفْ عَنَّا يَوْمًا مِّنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ (٤۹)
Those who are in the Fire will say to the keepers of hell, ‘Invoke your Lord to lighten for us [at least] a day’s punishment.’ (49)

Messages

1- In the Hereafter, the response to pleas for help from the disbelievers will be negative, and no one will carry the burden of another. (How can someone who is himself in the Fire save another?) «انّا كلٌّ فیها»

2- One day, the arrogant will humbly confess their own humiliation. «انّا كلّ فیها»

3- Wealth and status are not barriers to entering Hell. «الذّین استكبروا انّا كلّ فیها»

4- The judgment of Allah is beyond question or objection. «قد حكم بین العباد» (The arrogant rulers of today will be the condemned of tomorrow.)

5- In Ayah 44, we read: «انّ اللّه بصیر بالعباد» (Indeed, Allah is Seeing of the servants). Here we read: «ان اللّه قد حكم بین العباد» (Indeed, Allah has judged between the servants). This means the Judge is the One Who Himself has seen everything.

6- In the previous Ayah, it stated that the weak seek help from the arrogant; in this Ayah, it states that all of them together seek help from the guardian of Hell. «قال الذّین فى النار»

7- On the Day of Judgment, man will see the angels and speak with them. (The guardians of Hell are angels.) «و قال الذّین فى النار لخزنة جهنّم»

8- Hell has officials/keepers. «خزنة جهنّم»

9- The disbeliever's situation reaches a point where he seeks refuge with the very official of his torment. «قال الذّین فى النار لخزنة جهنّم»

10- Mitigation of the torment is only in the hand of Allah. «ادعوا ربّكم»

11- The inhabitants of Hell are despairing of eternal salvation and are content with the minimum reduction in the quantity and quality of the torment. «یخفّف - یوماً»

12- The torment of Hell is neither subject to suspension nor subject to mitigation. «یوماً من العذاب»

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَوَلَمْ تَكُ تَأْتِيكُمْ رُسُلُكُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ۖ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ ۚ قَالُوا۟ فَٱدْعُوا۟ ۗ وَمَا دُعَـٰٓؤُا۟ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ (٥٠)
They will say, ‘Had not your apostles brought you manifest proofs?’ They will say, ‘Yes.’ They will say, ‘Then invoke [Him] yourselves.’ But the invocations of the faithless only go awry. (50)

Key Points

Those who believe when they witness the wrath of Allah gain no benefit. "فلم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم لما رَاَوْا بأسنا" (102) Repentance at the moment of death is also useless. Pharaoh repented at the moment of drowning, but he was addressed: "Now you repent, while previously you disobeyed": "الآن و قد عصیت قبل" (103) Supplication and lamentation in Hell are also fruitless. "و ما دعاء الكافرین الا فى ضلال"

102) Ghafir, 85. 103) Yunus, 91.

Messages

1- Divine wrath and punishment occur after the completion of the proof (Hujjah). "اولم تك تأتیكم رسلكم"

2- The coming of the prophets is a permanent tradition (Sunnah) from the Lord. "تك تأتیكم"

3- The preacher must go to the people. "تأتیكم"

4- All prophets possessed miracles. "رسلكم بالبینات"

5- The Day of Judgment is the day of confession. "قالوا بلى"

6- The supplication of the disbeliever in Hell reaches nowhere. "و ما دعاء الكافرین"

Ayahs

إِنَّا لَنَنصُرُ رُسُلَنَا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَيَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلْأَشْهَـٰدُ (٥۱)
Indeed We shall help Our apostles and those who have faith in the life of the world and on the day when the witnesses rise up, (51)
يَوْمَ لَا يَنفَعُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ ۖ وَلَهُمُ ٱللَّعْنَةُ وَلَهُمْ سُوٓءُ ٱلدَّارِ (٥۲)
the day when the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, the curse will lie on them, and for them will be the ills of the [ultimate] abode. (52)

Key Points

We read in the previous verses that: The believer from Pharaoh's family initially concealed his faith, and then, alone, guided the people within Pharaoh's tyrannical regime. Pharaoh's family made efforts to turn him away from his belief, but Allah preserved his religion. This verse, which is the final part of that story, states: Do not think that Our support was exclusive to him; rather, Our tradition (Sunnah) is to support the Prophets and all believers, even if they are in hardship. In the book Ma'ad (The Resurrection), I have listed the names of the witnesses of the Day of Judgment from the Quran and narrations (Riwayat). The list of witnesses is as follows: Allah, the Prophets, the Imams, the angels, the earth, time, and the body parts, all of whom bear witness to our words and deeds. On the Day of Judgment, there are various stations and scenes; in one scene, the oppressors are not permitted to apologize, «و لا یؤذن لهم فیعتذرون»(104), and in this verse, He says: Even if they apologize, the apology will be of no benefit. «لا ینفع»

The Prophets and Divine Support

In the Quran, there is much talk about Divine support for the Prophets and believers, and we bring a portion of it as an instance of this verse which states: «انّا لننصر رسلنا و الذّین آمنوا فى الحیاة الدنیا» and the verse «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم»

  • Support for Noah عليه السلام: «فنجّیناه و من معه فى الفلك »(105) We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark.
  • Support for Abraham عليه السلام: «یا نار كونى برداً وسلاما»(106) O fire! Be cool and safe upon Abraham.
  • Support for Lot عليه السلام: «اذ نجیناه واهله اجمعین»(107) We saved him and all his family (except his wife).
  • Support for Joseph عليه السلام: «و كذلك مكنا لیوسف فى الارض یتبّوأ منها حیث یشاء»(108) And thus did We establish Joseph in the land, that he might settle therein wherever he willed.
  • Support for Shu'ayb عليه السلام: «نجّینا شعیباً و الذّین آمنوا معه»(109) We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him.
  • Support for Salih عليه السلام: «نجّینا صالحاً و الذّین آمنوا معه»(110) We saved Salih and those who believed with him.
  • Support for Hud عليه السلام: «نجینا هوداً والذّین آمنوا معه»(111) We saved Hud and those who believed with him.
  • Support for Jonah عليه السلام: «و نجیناه من الغم»(112) We saved Jonah from the distress.
  • Support for Moses عليه السلام: «و انجینا موسى و من معه اجمعین»(113) We saved Moses and all those who were with him.
  • Support for Jesus عليه السلام: «انّى متوفّیك و رافعك الىّ»(114) I will take you and raise you up to Myself.
  • Support for the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: «انّا فتحنا لك فتحاً مبینا»(115) Indeed, We have granted you a clear victory.
  • Support for the Believers: «و لقد نصركم اللّه ببدر و انتم اذلّة»(116) Indeed, Allah assisted you at Badr when you were weak. «ثم انزل اللّه سكینة على رسوله و على المؤمنین»(117) Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers. «هو الذّى انزل السكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین لیزدادوا ایمانا»(118) It is He Who sent down tranquility into the hearts of the believers that they might increase in faith.

In Surah Al-Anbiya, regarding the supplications of Prophets Noah, Jonah, Zechariah, and Job, we read: As soon as they called upon God, their prayers were answered: «فاستجبنا»(119) And in Surah Ash-Shura, Ayah 26, we read: «و یستجیب الذّین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» Allah answers the prayer of those who are people of faith and righteous deeds.

Divine support for the Prophets and believers manifests in various forms:

  • Sometimes by creating affection and inclination in hearts: «و لیربط على قلوبكم»(120), «یثبت اللّه الذّین آمنوا بالقول الثابت فى الحیاة الدنیا»(121)
  • Sometimes by answering prayers: «فاستجاب له ربّه»(122), «رب لا تذر على الارض من الكافرین دیّارا»(123)
  • Sometimes through miracles and argumentation: «ارسلنا رسلنا بالبینات»(124)
  • Sometimes by granting governance: «اتینا ال ابراهیم ملكا عظیما»(125)
  • Sometimes by victory in war: «و لقد نصركم اللّه فى مواطن كثیرة»(126)
  • Sometimes through tranquility (Sakina) and peace of heart: «فَاَنزل اللّه سكینته علیه و ایدّه بجنود لم تروها»(127)
  • Sometimes by destroying the enemy or taking vengeance upon him: «فَاَغرقناهم»(128)
  • Sometimes through unseen aid and the descent of angels: «الن یكفیكم ان یمدكم ربّكم بثلاثة الاف من الملائكة منزلین»(129)
  • Sometimes by casting terror into the hearts of the enemy: «و قذف فى قلوبهم الرعب»(130)
  • Sometimes by spreading the idea, culture, and followers: «لیظهره على الدین كله»(131)
  • Sometimes by saving them from dangers: «فانجیناه و اصحاب السفینة»(132)
  • And sometimes by neutralizing plots and deceits: «و انّ اللّه موهن كید الكافرین»(133)

104) Al-Mursalat, 36. 105) Yunus, 73. 106) Al-Anbiya, 69. 107) As-Saffat, 134. 108) Yusuf, 56. 109) Hud, 94. 110) Hud, 66. 111) Hud, 58. 112) Al-Anbiya, 88. 113) Ash-Shu'ara, 65. 114) Al 'Imran, 55. 115) Al-Fath, 1. 116) Al 'Imran, 123. 117) At-Tawbah, 26. 118) Al-Fath, 4. 119) Al-Anbiya, 76, 84, 88 and 90. 120) Al-An'am, 11. 121) Ibrahim, 27. 122) Yusuf. 123) Nuh, 26. 124) Al-Hadid, 25. 125) An-Nisa, 54. 126) At-Tawbah, 25. 127) At-Tawbah, 40. 128) Al-A'raf, 136. 129) Al 'Imran, 124. 130) Al-Hashr, 2. 131) As-Saff, 9. 132) Al-'Ankabut, 15. 133) Al-Anfal, 18.

Messages

  1. Divine support for the Prophets and believers is among the Divine traditions. (The victory of truth over falsehood is one of Allah's promises). «انّا لننصر رسلنا»
  2. Let us not doubt Divine support. «انّا لننصر» («انّا» and the letter lam indicate emphasis)
  3. We should give motivation, hope, and consolation to the believers through Allah's definite promises. «انّا لننصر رسلنا...»
  4. Divine support is comprehensive, covering the neutralization of enemies' plots, the increase of followers, and so on. «لننصر» is stated absolutely (unrestricted).
  5. Glad tidings and warnings side-by-side are constructive. «لننصر رسلنا... لا ینفع الظالمین»
  6. Faith gives value to a person, placing him alongside the Prophets and making him eligible for Divine support. «رسلنا و الذّین آمنوا»
  7. The hardships and tortures of this world last no more than a few hours or days. Even in this world, the adherents of truth are victorious.(134) «لننصر رسلنا و الذّین آمنوا»
  8. Man is always in need of Divine support and is never independent of His grace. «فى الحیاة الدنیا و یوم یقوم الاشهاد»
  9. Divine support is continuous. «فى الحیاة الدنیا و یوم یقوم الاشهاد»
  10. Although Allah's witnessing alone is sufficient, the greater the number of witnesses, the more honored the believers will be and the more ashamed the transgressors will become. «یقوم الاشهاد»
  11. On the Day of Judgment, the witnesses are numerous. «الاشهاد»
  12. Those who do not have faith are oppressors. («الظالمین» is in contrast to «الذّین آمنوا»)

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَأَوْرَثْنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ (٥۳)
Certainly We gave guidance to Moses and We gave the Children of Israel the Book as a legacy, (53)
هُدًى وَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (٥٤)
as guidance and admonition for those who possess intellect. (54)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the discussion was about the victory (support) of the prophets and the believers. Here, it seems that the example of "رُسُلَنَا" (Our Messengers) is presented as Moses, and the example of "الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا" (those who believed) is presented as the Children of Israel. "الْأَلْبَابِ" (Al-Albāb) is the plural of "لُبّ" (Lubb), meaning pure intellect or unadulterated wisdom.

Messages

  1. Guidance is a gift from Allah. "إِيتَاء" (Ītā’) means "to grant." "آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْهُدَىٰ" (We gave Moses the guidance).

  2. The guide must Himself have been guided previously. "آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْهُدَىٰ" (We gave Moses the guidance).

  3. All people, even the prophets, need Divine guidance. "آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْهُدَىٰ" (We gave Moses the guidance).

  4. If guidance is to endure, it must be written down; the secret to the permanence of Divine religions lies in the heavenly books. "آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْهُدَىٰ وَأَوْرَثْنَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْكِتَابَ" (We gave Moses the guidance and We made the Children of Israel inherit the Book).

  5. If guidance is not accompanied by remembrance (admonition), it will be forgotten. "هُدًى وَذِكْرَىٰ" (a guidance and a reminder).

  6. Understanding heavenly knowledge requires wisdom and piety. "هُدًى لِلْمُتَّقِينَ" (a guidance for the pious) (135), "هُدًى وَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ" (a guidance and a reminder for those of understanding).

  7. Intellect does not render man independent of revelation; rather, both complement each other. "هُدًى وَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ" (a guidance and a reminder for those of understanding).

  8. The elements of felicity are several things: A) Divine grace and guidance. "وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ" (And certainly We gave Moses). B) Infallible leadership. "مُوسَىٰ" (Moses). C) Heavenly law. "الْكِتَابَ" (the Book). D) Admonition and avoidance of heedlessness. "ذِكْرَىٰ" (a reminder). E) Pure and unadulterated intellect and wisdom. "لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ" (for those of understanding).

  9. Awareness and taking admonition are signs of sound intellect and pure wisdom. "ذِكْرَىٰ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ" (a reminder for those of understanding).

  10. The knowledge contained in the heavenly books conforms to intellect and wisdom. "ذِكْرَىٰ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ" (a reminder for those of understanding). (135) Al-Baqarah, 2.

Ayah

فَٱصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنۢبِكَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ بِٱلْعَشِىِّ وَٱلْإِبْكَـٰرِ (٥٥)
So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. Plead [Allah] for forgiveness of your sin, and celebrate the praise of your Lord morning and evening. (55)

Key Points

Rational and transmitted proofs attest that the Prophets are infallible, because if they were not infallible, absolute and unquestioning obedience to them would not be obligatory, and obedience to them would be conditional, just as obedience to parents is conditional upon them not inviting the child to polytheism or deviation. This is from one side; on the other side, the reason for our need for a Prophet and Imam is that He should prevent us from deviation, and if He Himself were prone to error, Allah's proof upon us would not be complete, while the Quran states: «و للّه الحجّة البالغة»(136) In any case, a Messenger about whom Allah says: "Obedience to him is obedience to Me, following him is following Me, and harming him is harming Me," must be infallible. Therefore, in cases like this Ayah, where the Prophet's sin is mentioned, we must say: the meaning of sin is Tark al-Awla (abandoning the superior action), meaning He abandoned the superior action, not the obligatory action.

Pay attention to a few examples: If, in a gathering where respected people are seated, someone needs to stretch out his leg due to pain, he apologizes to all attendees. If a television speaker coughs, he apologizes to the viewers. If a host does not prepare first-rate food, he apologizes to the guest, while we all know that a sick person stretching his leg, a speaker coughing, or preparing average food is not a sin. But because a person considers himself in the presence of viewers and dignitaries, or does not consider his food worthy of the guest, or feels he should have made a greater effort, he apologizes. Thus, every apology is not a sign of wrongdoing, but sometimes it is out of courtesy and attention to being in the presence of great figures.

Infallibility and Istighfar (Seeking Forgiveness) of the Prophets

If you enter a large hall with a dim light, you will only see large objects, but if you enter with a strong spotlight, you will see a date pit or something smaller than that. If the light of faith in a person is weak, he only sees major sins, but if the light of faith is greater within him, he will also see small slips and will distance himself from them. The Divine Prophets, due to having complete faith and high piety, also distanced themselves from small sins. For this reason, we say they were infallible.

Sometimes the address of the Quranic verses is to the person of the Prophet, but others are intended. In Ayah 23 of Surah Al-Isra, Allah addresses His Prophet: «اما یبلغن عندك الكبر احدهما او كلاهما فلا تقل لهما اُف» (If one of them or both of them reach old age in your presence, do not say 'Uff' to them). While we all know that the beloved Prophet of Islam had lost His parents in childhood, leaving no room for the address "عندك الكبر" (reach old age in your presence). Therefore, the address "عندك" is directed to His Ummah (community). In the Ayah under discussion, which states: «و استغفر لذنبك» (And seek forgiveness for your sin), although the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is addressed, the primary audience is the sinners of the Prophet's Ummah, who must seek forgiveness for their sins. And even if the addressee is the Prophet Himself, the intention is that He should seek forgiveness for the sins of the Ummah.

Istighfar (Seeking Forgiveness) of the Prophets in the Quran

In the Noble Quran, verses speak of the Prophets' seeking forgiveness. Here we mention some of them:

  • Istighfar of Prophet Adam: «ربّنا ظلمنا انفسنا و ان لم تغفر لنا»(137) Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us, we will surely be among the losers.
  • Istighfar of Prophet Moses: «ربّ انى ظلمت نفسى فاغفرلى»(138) My Lord! I have wronged myself, so forgive me.
  • Istighfar of Prophet Solomon: «ربّ اغفرلى وهب لى ملكاً»(139) My Lord! Forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me.
  • Istighfar of Prophet Noah: «ربّ اغفرلى ولوالدىّ»(140) My Lord! Forgive me and my parents.
  • Istighfar of Prophet David: «و ظنّ داود انما فتنّاه فاستغفر ربّه»(141) And David realized that We had tested him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord.
  • Istighfar of Prophet Jonah: «سبحانك انى كنت من الظالمین»(142) Jonah (in the belly of the fish) said: Glory be to You! I have been among the wrongdoers.
  • Istighfar of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله: «و استغفر لذنبك»(143) Seek forgiveness for your sin.

136) Al-An'am, 149. 137) Al-A'raf, 23. 138) Al-Qasas, 16. 139) Sad, 35. 140) Nuh, 28. 141) Sad, 24. 142) Al-Anbiya, 87. 143) Ghafir, 55.

Messages

1- Considering Allah's promise regarding the help of the Prophets, «انّا لننصر رسلنا...» (Indeed, We will support Our messengers...), this Ayah says: Be steadfast. «فاصبر»

2- The motivation for patience is attaining the Divine promises. «فاصبر انّ وعداللّه حقّ»

3- The Prophet is also commanded to seek forgiveness (Istighfar). «و استغفر لذنبك»

4- Glorifying Allah has greater value if it is accompanied by His praise. «و سبّح بحمد ربّك»

5- The element of time plays a role in supplication and worship. «سبّح... بالعشىّ و الابكار» (Just as He says in other verses: «و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون»(144), «اقم الصلاة لدلوك الشمس»(145))

6- Glorification (Tasbeeh) and praise (Hamd) are means of growth and training, and a cause for strengthening Monotheism (Tawhid), only when they are performed morning and evening, and are continuous, not momentary. «بالعشىّ و الابكار»

144) Al 'Imran, 17. 145) Al-Isra, 78.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُجَـٰدِلُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطَـٰنٍ أَتَىٰهُمْ ۙ إِن فِى صُدُورِهِمْ إِلَّا كِبْرٌ مَّا هُم بِبَـٰلِغِيهِ ۚ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ (٥٦)
Indeed those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them—there is [an ambition for] greatness in their breasts, which they will never attain. So seek the protection of Allah; indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing. (56)

Key Points

Islam gives primary value to intention (niyyah), spirit, motivation, and what passes through the heart. For this reason, the best deed, if not accompanied by sincerity (ikhlas), is worthless and void. In Surah 'Abasa, ten consecutive verses criticize someone who frowned (made his face grim) at a blind man. (While for a blind person, a frown or a smile makes no difference, as he sees neither.) But the Quran severely criticizes it because the root of frowning is contempt (humiliation). Yes, Islam pays more attention to the inner self and the heart than to the outward appearance. In this verse too, the discussion is about the arrogance and pride that resides in the hearts of the arrogant.

Messages

1- What is in the jar is what spills out of it. The source of illogical disputes is inner arrogance. «یجادلون.... فى صدورهم... كبر»

2- The motivation for disputing the truth is self-aggrandizement, not logic. «بغیر سلطان اتاهم ان فى صدورهم الاّ كبر»

3- If a person does not guard himself, arrogance will consume his entire being, such that there is nothing in his heart except arrogance. «ان فى صدورهم الاّ كبر»

4- Whoever insists stubbornly based on arrogance and lacks logic will not reach his goal, which is leadership and mastery. «ما هم ببالغیه»

Learn humility if you seek grace, The high ground never drinks water.

5- Dealing with the arrogant has no remedy except seeking refuge in Allah. «فاستعذ باللَّه»

6- We should seek refuge in One Who hears and sees (and naturally has the power to help and neutralize plots). «انّه هو السمیع البصیر»

Ayah

لَخَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ خَلْقِ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٥۷)
The creation of the heavens and the earth is surely more prodigious than the creation of mankind, but most people do not know. (57)

Messages

1- If we look at the greatness of existence, we will never see ourselves as great. «ان فى صدورهم الاّ كِبر... لخلق السموات...»

2- Arrogant individuals must be humbled. «لخلق السموات و الارض اكبر» Why do you show arrogance?!

3- A look that lacks admonition is ignorance. «لخلق السموات... لا یعلمون»

4- The root of arrogance is ignorance and heedlessness. «ولكن اكثر النّاس لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَلَا ٱلْمُسِىٓءُ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٥۸)
The blind one and the seer are not equal, neither are the evildoers and those who have faith and do righteous deeds. Little is the admonition that you take! (58)

Messages

1- Arrogance (Takabbur) is the greatest barrier to seeing the truth and accepting the truth. (The arrogant person is like the blind). «ما یستوى الاعمى و البصیر»

2- Faith and insight precede action. «آمنوا و عملوا...»

3- What is central is knowledge and remembrance (Tadhakkur), not statistics and figures. «اكثر النّاس لا یعلمون... قلیلاً ما تتذكّرون»

4- A person who is indifferent to the truths is blind. «الاعمى... قلیلاً ما تتذكّرون»

5- The masses of people do not know, and the group that is insightful and takes heed is small. «اكثر النّاس لا یعلمون... قلیلاً ما تتذكّرون»

6- In the previous Ayah, we read that: "Most people do not know." In this Ayah, He said: "Few individuals remember and take heed." Therefore, knowing is not the main point; being vigilant is the main point. «قلیلاً ما تتذكّرون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَـَٔاتِيَةٌ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهَا وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۹)
Indeed the Hour is bound to come; there is no doubt in it. But most people do not believe. (59)

Key Points

Since the Resurrection (Qiyamah) will be established suddenly and quickly, it has been named "الساعة" (The Hour). Wherever it appears with the definite article alif and lam, it means the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). But without the definite article, it means time, whether a short time, like «لا یستقدمون ساعة و لا یستأخرون»(146), meaning their death will not be delayed or advanced even for a moment, or absolute time, such as «فى ساعة العسرة»(147). Although some people have expressed doubt and considered the Resurrection improbable, considering Allah's Wisdom, Justice, Power, and Knowledge, it is not appropriate to doubt it. «لاریب فیها» (There is no doubt in it).

146) A'raf, 34. 147) Tawbah, 117.

Messages

1- In response to the insistence of some who ask, "When is the time of the Resurrection?" the Quran never discusses its timing; what is emphasized is the principle of the Resurrection's occurrence. «انّ الساعة لآتیة»

2- Remembering the Resurrection is the best means of remembrance (tadhakkur) and removing heedlessness. «قلیلاً ما تتذكّرون - انّ الساعة لآتیة»

3- In the face of the disbelievers' skepticism regarding the Resurrection, you must state the message of truth with certainty. «لاریب فیها»

4- People's doubt about the occurrence of the Resurrection stems from their ignorance of Allah's knowledge and power. «اكثر النّاس لایعلمون»

Ayah

وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ٱدْعُونِىٓ أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِى سَيَدْخُلُونَ جَهَنَّمَ دَاخِرِينَ (٦٠)
Your Lord has said, ‘Call Me, and I will hear you!’ Indeed those who are disdainful of My worship will enter hell in utter humiliation. (60)

Key Points

| Question: Although Allah has promised acceptance, «اُدعونى استجب لكم» ("Call upon Me, I will respond to you"), why are some of our supplications not answered? Answer: A) Certain actions, such as sin, oppression, consuming unlawful earnings (a forbidden morsel), and failing to forgive those who apologize to us, prevent the acceptance of supplication. B) Sometimes the acceptance of our supplication disrupts the order of creation, like the student who, in a geography exam, when asked whether sea levels are higher or mountains, mistakenly wrote, "Sea levels are higher!" and then asked Allah to swap the two so he could pass! In any case, just as Allah is All-Powerful, He is also All-Wise (Hakim). C) Sometimes that specific supplication is not answered, but something similar to it is. D) Sometimes the effect of the supplication yields results in the future of the person himself, or his family and descendants, or on the Day of Judgment, and is not answered immediately. Any request that is not answered is not truly a du'a (supplication), because du'a means seeking good (khayr), and many of our desires are evil (sharr), not good. Manners of Supplication in the Quran 1. Supplication must be accompanied by faith and righteous deeds. «یستجیب الذّین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»(148) 2. It must be with sincerity. «فادعوا اللّه مخلصین له الدین»(149) 3. It must be with humility and secretly. «ادعوا ربّكم تضرعاً و خُفیة»(150) 4. It must be with fear and hope. «و ادعوه خوفاً و طمعاً»(151) 5. It must be at specific times. «یدعون ربّهم بالغداة و العشى»(152)

148) Shura, 26. 149) Mu'min, 14. 150) A'raf, 55. 151) A'raf, 56. 152) An'am, 52.

Messages

1- Dialogue with Allah is the key to human growth and refinement. «قال ربّكم اُدعونى»

2- There is a close relationship between human supplication and the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. (Most supplications in the Quran are accompanied by "Rabbana" [Our Lord]). «ربّكم اُدعونى»

3- Allah has asked us to supplicate to Him. «قال ربّكم اُدعونى»

4- In supplication, we must call upon Allah alone, not Allah and others alongside Him. «اُدعونى»

5- Allah knows our needs, but the command to supplicate indicates that our request has effects. «ادعونى»

6- There is no gap between supplication and acceptance. «ادعونى استجب» (Even the particle fa' or thumma is not used. If He had said: fa-astajib, it would mean, "I will answer after a short time," and if He had said: thumma astajib, it would mean, "I will answer after a long time.")

7- There is no intermediary involved in supplication and acceptance. «اُدعونى استجب»

8- The acceptance of supplication is a Divine promise. «اُدعونى استجب لكم»

9- If a supplication is not answered, it must not have been beneficial for us, because He said: «استجب لكم» (I will respond to you), meaning I will answer what is beneficial for you.

10- Failure to supplicate is a sign of arrogance (takabbur). «یستكبرون»

11- Supplication is the cure for arrogance. «یستكبرون عن عبادتى»

12- Supplication is worship itself. «یستكبرون عن عبادتى»

13- Supplication is a specific and Divine form of worship. «عبادتى»

14- Allah’s punishment is just and proportionate to the type of human behavior and action. Arrogance today leads to humiliation tomorrow. «یستكبرون - داخرین»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (٦۱)
It is Allah who made the night for you, that you may rest in it, and the day to provide visibility. Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, but most people do not give thanks. (61)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we were invited to supplication (Du'a) and worship ('Ibadah). In this Ayah, Allah mentions a part of His blessings to revive the spirit of knowledge, love, worship, and supplication within us. Several things are introduced in the Qur'an as means of tranquility (Sakinah). We present some examples of them:

A) The remembrance of Allah. «ألا بذكر اللّه تطمئنّ القلوب»(153) Beware, only with the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest. B) The supplication of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. «ان صلاتك سكن لهم»(154) Indeed, your prayer (supplication) is a source of tranquility for them. C) Divine assistance. «هو الذّى انزل السكینة فى قلوب المؤمنین»(155) He is the One Who sent down tranquility (Sakinah) into the hearts of the believers. D) Spouse. «لتسكنوا الیها»(156) Allah made for you spouses so that you may find tranquility with her. E) Home. «الذّى جعل لكم من بیوتكم سكنا»(157) The One Who made your houses a place of rest for you. F) Night, which is a suitable time for resting and comfort. «جعل لكم الّیل لتسكنوا فیه»

153) Ra'd, 28. 154) Tawbah, 103. 155) Fath, 4. 156) Rum, 21. 157) Nahl, 80.

Messages

1- Worship and supplication are due only to the One Who is the Creator of night and day and the Provider of our needs. «اُدعونى... اللّه الذّى جعل لكم الّیل...»

2- Night is for tranquility and day is for vitality and effort. «الّیل لتسكنوا فیه و النّهار مبصراً»

3- Rest is a prerequisite for work. (The phrase «لتسكنوا» precedes «و النّهار مبصرة»).

4- Night and day together constitute a blessing. «الّیل - النّهار»

5- Allah's favor (Fadl) in this world encompasses both the believer and the disbeliever. «لذو فضل على النّاس»

6- No one has a claim upon Allah; what He gives is His grace. «لذو فضل»

7- A sign of His favor is that even though He knows most people are ungrateful, He still does not withhold His grace. «لذو فضل على النّاس و لكن اكثر النّاس لا یشكرون»

8- Majority is not a sign of righteousness (or truth). «اكثر النّاس لا یشكرون»

9- Allah is not waiting for our gratitude and thanks. It is our duty to thank Him. «لذو فضل على النّاس و لكن اكثر النّاس لا یشكرون»

10- Night and day are two blessings that deserve gratitude. «جعل لكم الّیل... و النّهار... ولكن اكثر النّاس لا یشكرون»

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ (٦۲)
That is Allah, your Lord, the creator of all things, there is no god except Him. Then where do you stray? (62)
كَذَٰلِكَ يُؤْفَكُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ (٦۳)
Those who were used to impugning the signs of Allah are thus made to go astray. (63)

Key Points

| «Ifk» means deviation from the path of truth. A lie is also called «ifk» because it is contrary to the truth. «Jahd» is the denial of something we believe in our hearts, or the affirmation of something we do not believe in our hearts. (158)

158) Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- First, show the truth, then criticize. «ذلكم اللّه ربّكم... انّى تؤفكون»

2- Whoever does not commit his heart to the source of existence becomes deviated. «ربّكم - تؤفكون»

3- The One worthy of worship is He who exercises Lordship (Rububiyyah) over people and is the Creator of all things. «ربّكم خالق... لا اله الاّ هو»

4- Manufacturers and producers demonstrate their creativity using the tools and resources provided by Allah. Therefore, the true Creator of everything is He. «خالق كل شى ء»

5- Except for the path of Allah and belief in His Lordship and His Oneness, any path we take is a deviation. «فانّى تؤفكون»

6- Belittle the misguided and arrogant deviants so that they do not think they are superior individuals. «كذلك یؤفك الذّین...»

7- Denial of the truth is the prelude to deviation. «تؤفكون... كانوا... یجحدون»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ قَرَارًا وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بِنَآءً وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦٤)
It is Allah who made for you the earth an abode and the sky a canopy. He formed you and perfected your forms, and provided you with all the good things. That is Allah, your Lord! Blessed is Allah, Lord of all the worlds! (64)

Key Points

A few verses prior, the discussion was about temporal blessings like night and day. This verse addresses spatial blessings like the earth and the sky. For knowing Allah, attention is necessary both to the cosmic and natural signs (آیات آفاقى) — «الارض... و السماء» — and to the internal signs (آیات انفسى) — «صوّركم... رزقكم...». Regarding the creation of man and the granting of the soul to him, Allah congratulated Himself and said: «فتبارك اللّه احسن الخالقین» (Blessed is Allah, the best of creators). In this verse, too, where the subject of the excellent creation of man is raised, Allah congratulates Himself and says: «فتبارك اللّه ربّ العالمین» (Blessed is Allah, the Lord of the worlds).

Messages

1- The Earth was initially restless and shaky; Allah made it tranquil for human habitation. «جعل لكم الارض قرارا»

2- Creation is purposeful. «لكم الارض»

3- Despite all its movements, the Earth is tranquil. «قرارا»

4- Among all creatures, Allah has bestowed the best form upon man. «صوّركم فاحسن صوركم»

5- The beauty of the countenance is one of the divine blessings. «فاحسن صوركم»

6- What Allah has decreed as our provision (rizq) are tayyibat (good, pleasing, and pure things). If man utilizes things other than tayyibat, it is due to his own poor choice and obstinacy. «رزقكم من الطّیّبات»

7- The beautiful physique and pure provision are manifestations of divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «فاحسن صوركم و رزقكم... ذلكم اللّه ربّكم»

8- The tranquility of the Earth, the structure of the sky, your creation, and the provision of pure things—all of this is for your growth and development. «ذلكم اللّه ربّكم»

9- The existence of Allah is the source of blessing and mercy. «فتبارك اللّه»

10- The entire existence, under the nurturing care (tarbiyah) of Allah, is moving toward perfection. «ربّ العالمین»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَٱدْعُوهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ ۗ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦٥)
He is the Living One, there is no god except Him. So supplicate Him, putting exclusive faith in Him. All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds. (65)

Messages

1- True life belongs to Allah. «هو الحىّ» (The life of others is limited, perishable, and dependent.)

2- First comes recognition and knowledge, then supplication and worship. «احسن صوركم... خلقكم... هو الحى... لا اله الاّ هو... فادعوه مخلصین»

3- The Worshipped must be alive. «هو الحىّ لا اله الاّ هو»

4- If there is no deity but Him, then we should worship Him alone. «لا اله الاّ هو فادعوه مخلصین»

5- Among the etiquette of supplication is praising the Lord. «فادعوه... الحمدللّه...»

6- There is a close relationship between supplication and Lordship (Rububiyyah). «فادعوه... ربّ العالمین»

7- Allah's Lordship is the reason for praising Him. («الحمدللّه ربّ العالمین»

8- Existence is moving towards perfection. «ربّ العالمین»

Ayah

۞ قُلْ إِنِّى نُهِيتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ لَمَّا جَآءَنِىَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ مِن رَّبِّى وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦٦)
Say, ‘I have been forbidden to worship those whom you invoke besides Allah, as manifest proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds.’ (66)

Key Points

This Ayah and the two Ayahs preceding it conclude with the blessed phrase "ربّ العالمین" (Lord of the Worlds). Ayah 64, after proving the essence of Allah, stated: "ربّ العالمین". Ayah 65, after affirming Monotheism (Tawhid) and sincerity (Ikhlas), stated: "ربّ العالمین". And this Ayah, after negating all forms of polytheism (Shirk), presents "ربّ العالمین". Yes, the natural and logical progression of knowing God is that first we know Him, then we believe in His Oneness, and then we distance ourselves from everything that hinders His Oneness and Tawhid.

Messages

1- Disappoint your enemies. «قل انّى نُهیتُ»

2- Revelation was conveyed to the people without any omission or addition. «قل انّى نُهیتُ»

3- The axis is evidence (Dalil) and clear proof (Bayyinah), not the expectations of the people or the environment. (When clear proofs exist, there is no room left for submitting to polytheism/Shirk). «جاءنى البینات»

4- Divine command and prohibition are based on logic and reasoning. «نهیتُ... لمّا جاءنى البینات»

5- The stances taken by the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are based on revelation. «نهیتُ - امرتُ»

6- Tawalli (loving/befriending) and Tabarri (disassociating/disavowing) must be side by side. (Unless we distance ourselves from all forms of polytheism/Shirk, we will not become true monotheists). «نهیتُ ان اعبد الذّین تدعون... و امرت ان اسلم»

7- Submission is worthy only before Him who is the Lord of the Worlds. «اسلم لربّ العالمین»

8- True worship is submission to Allah. («اسلم لربّ العالمین» is used instead of «اعبد لربّ العالمین»).

9- Submission to Allah is the cause of human growth and development. «اسلم لربّ العالمین»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلًا ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوٓا۟ أَشُدَّكُمْ ثُمَّ لِتَكُونُوا۟ شُيُوخًا ۚ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلِتَبْلُغُوٓا۟ أَجَلًا مُّسَمًّى وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (٦۷)
It is He who created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then from a clinging mass, then He brings you forth as infants, then [He nurtures you] so that you may come of age, and then that you may become aged—though there are some of you who die earlier—and complete a specified term, and so that you may exercise your reason. (67)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah has described the creation, life, and death of man in seven stages. The creation of man from dust (soil) has been interpreted in two ways: A) The first man was created from dust. B) Existing human beings originate from sperm (nutfah), and sperm originates from food, and food originates from the nutritional elements of the earth. In the preceding verses, reference was made to supplication (du'a), knowledge (ma'rifah), sincerity (ikhlas), gratitude (shukr), and submission (taslim). This Ayah serves as preparation for reaching those stages.

Messages

1- The beginning of human creation is from dust, not from evolved animals. «خلقكم من تراب»

2- Divine power creates a living human being from lifeless dust. «خلقكم من تراب»

3- Divine power creates a thinking human being from dust. «من تراب - تعقلون»

4- The stages of creation occur gradually. «ثمّ...ثمّ...ثمّ»

5- The creation of man from dust and sperm is by the hand of Allah, but it is we who must reach a certain point through effort. «ثمّ لتبلغوا»

6- Human life passes through stages of development. «تراب - طفلاً - شیوخاً - یتوفّى»

7- Taking the soul (life) is by the hand of Allah. «یتوفّى»

8- Death is not annihilation; it is the taking of the soul from the body. «یتوفّى»

9- Human development has no limits. (Considering that the extent of growth is not specified in the Ayah, everyone can reach stages of strength and intensity.) «لتبلغوا اشدّكم»

10- The lifespan of every person is predetermined. «اجلاً مسمّى »

11- The best context for reasoning (ta'aqqul) is the creation of man himself. «من تراب ثمّ من نطفة... لعلّكم تعقلون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۖ فَإِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (٦۸)
It is He who gives life and brings death. When He decides on a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is. (68)

Key Points

In the Quran, the phrase "كن فیكون" (Be, and it is) has appeared eight times, which is the symbol of Allah's absolute power. The intent of the Divine saying in this verse is Allah's creative will for the existence of a matter, not that He needs to speak. "فانّما یقول له كن فیكون"

Messages

  1. Giving life and causing death is solely in His hand. "هو الذّى"

  2. His power regarding giving life and causing death is uniform. "یحى و یمیت"

  3. Allah's power is absolute, and no matter is outside the scope of Allah's power. (The word "امراً" means 'any matter').

  4. Allah's will is certain and definite and cannot be defeated. "كن فیكون"

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يُجَـٰدِلُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ أَنَّىٰ يُصْرَفُونَ (٦۹)
Have you not regarded those who dispute the signs of Allah, where they are being led away [from Allah’s way]? (69)
ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَبِمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا بِهِۦ رُسُلَنَا ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (۷٠)
—Those who deny the Book and what we have sent with Our apostles. Soon they will know (70)

Key Points

| In this Surah, the topic of unjustifiable dispute (jidal) is raised three times: a) In Ayah 35, it is introduced as a great sin and a cause for the sealing of hearts. b) In Ayah 56, the motive for the dispute is identified as the arrogance (takabbur) that resides in the hearts of the disputants. c) In this Ayah, the deviation and going astray of the disputants is mentioned. (159)

(159) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of stubborn past nations is a means of lesson and a source of tranquility for the nations of today. «الم تَرَ»

2- Dispute (jidal), according to the verses of the Quran, is going astray. «یُجادلون... یُصرفون»

3- In the method of propagation, warning also holds a positive place. «فسوف یعلمون»

Ayahs

إِذِ ٱلْأَغْلَـٰلُ فِىٓ أَعْنَـٰقِهِمْ وَٱلسَّلَـٰسِلُ يُسْحَبُونَ (۷۱)
when they are dragged [with] iron collars and chains around their necks (71)
فِى ٱلْحَمِيمِ ثُمَّ فِى ٱلنَّارِ يُسْجَرُونَ (۷۲)
into scalding waters and then set aflame in the Fire. (72)

Key Points

«أغلال» (Aghlāl) is the plural of «غُلّ» (ghull), meaning a pendant or collar placed around someone's neck as a sign of humiliation. «سَلاسل» (Salāsil) is the plural of «سلسلة» (silsilah), meaning chain, and «یسحبون» (yusḥabūn) means being dragged upon the ground. «یسجرون» (yusjarūn) means the kindling or inflaming of fire.

What causes warning, education, and the emergence of piety (taqwa) is the depiction of the countenance of the Day of Judgment and the types of punishments that have been described through revelation. Philosophical faith, where a person believes in the Resurrection only generally through the concepts of justice and wisdom, will not have these effects. Just as proving God through the negation of circularity (dawr) and infinite regress (tasalsul) does not lead to piety and effort, even though these intellectual discussions are effective in their proper place.

In this Ayah, various types of punishment for the disbelievers are mentioned: a) Physical torture in chains and fetters. «الاغلال...» b) The manner of being taken to Hell, which is humiliating. «یسحبون» c) First in boiling water, then in the fire of Hell. «فى الحمیم ثم فى النار» d) The blazing fire of Hell, which never cools down or diminishes. «یسجرون» e) Questioning them about their imaginary partners, which causes them shame. «قیل لهم این»

Messages

1- Stubborn individuals realize their deviation when they see the punishment. «فسوف یعلمون اذ الاغلال...»

2- Disputing and denying the truth causes man to be afflicted by various types of punishment. «یجادلون... كذبوا... اذ الاغلال»

3- Man's arrogance and seeking greatness will eventually turn into humiliation. (Arrogance, rebellion, pride, and polytheism will not go unanswered). «الاغلال... یسحبون...»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ (۷۳)
Then they will be told, ‘Where are they whom you used to worship as deities (73)
مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالُوا۟ ضَلُّوا۟ عَنَّا بَل لَّمْ نَكُن نَّدْعُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ شَيْـًٔا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۷٤)
besides Allah?’ They will say, ‘They have forsaken us. No, we did not invoke anything before.’ That is how Allah leads the faithless astray. (74)

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the day when realities are revealed and polytheism (shirk) is rendered ineffective. «ضلوا عنّا»

2- Everything besides Allah is perishable/destructible. «ضلوا عنّا»

3- The events of the Day of Judgment will confuse the polytheists (mushrikeen) to such an extent that they will say one time: Our deities have vanished, and another time they will say: We never worshipped any deity at all. «ضلوا عنا... لم نكن ندعوا»

4- Allah does not lead anyone astray without reason. (Divine misguidance is the punishment for the disbelief of the disbelievers). «یضل اللّه الكافرین»

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَمْرَحُونَ (۷٥)
‘That [punishment] is because you used to boast unduly on the earth and because you used to strut. (75)
ٱدْخُلُوٓا۟ أَبْوَٰبَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ (۷٦)
Enter the gates of hell, to remain in it [forever].’ Evil is the [final] abode of the arrogant. (76)

Key Points

Farah (فرح) refers to natural joy, while marah (مرح) refers to excessive joy that causes human arrogance and intoxication. Mathwa (مثوى) means residence accompanied by permanence, meaning an eternal and everlasting abode.

Islam and Recreation Islam is the religion of nature (fitrah) and does not oppose recreation and joy, which are instinctive human desires. What is criticized are inappropriate recreations and joys, some instances of which the Quran mentions:

  • The joy of a group who were happy because they did not go to the battlefield. «فرح المخلفون بمقعدهم خلاف رسول اللّه»(160)
  • Joy due to worldly achievements accompanied by heedlessness of the Hereafter. «فرحوا بالحیاة الدنیا»(161)
  • Joy due to acquiring wealth. Qarun (Korah) was told: «لا تفرح»
  • Joy due to hardships that befall the believers. «اِن تصبكم سیئة فرحوا بها»(162)
  • Joy due to possessing knowledge. «فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم»(163)

However, if joy is based on truth, Divine grace, service to people, and achieving perfection, it is not only good but a value. «قل بفضل اللّه و برحمته فبذلك فلیفرحوا»(164)

160) Tawbah, 81. 161) Ra‘d, 26. 162) Āl ‘Imrān, 120. 163) Ghāfir, 83. 164) Yūnus, 58.

Messages

1- The reasons for Divine punishment and wrath must be stated, so that the criminal does not feel entitled, and others may be warned. «ذلكم بما كنتم»

2- Intoxication and inappropriate recreation are causes of Divine wrath. «كنتم تفرحون»

3- Recreation must be based on truth, limited so as not to cause heedlessness, and within the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid). «تفرحون... بغیر الحقّ»

4- Today's inappropriate joys, which are based on transgression, usurpation, killing, looting, and mocking values and sanctities, will one day turn into chains, shackles, and entry into Hell accompanied by humiliation and disgrace. «ذلكم بما كنتم تفرحون... بغیر الحقّ»

5- Joy is commendable if it is based on truth, but it is criticized if it is based on falsehood. «تفرحون... بغیر الحقّ»

6- The gates of Hell are numerous. «ادخلوا ابواب»

Ayah

فَٱصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۚ فَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ (۷۷)
So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. Whether We show you a part of what We promise them, or take you away [before that], [in any case] they will be brought back to Us. (77)

Key Points

In this Surah, while the dispute and contention of the disbelievers have been mentioned three times, (165) Allah tells the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: "So be patient, for the promise of Allah is true." Both threatening the enemies and consoling the friends are necessary. The previous verses were for threatening the contentious disbelievers, and this verse is for consoling the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the believers.

The Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله Conduct with People

All of the Prophet's interactions with people were according to the command of Allah, not personal preference, and these interactions varied in different situations and with different individuals. The verses existing in this regard can be divided into two categories: The first category: Verses that speak about calm conduct and interaction with people. The second category: Verses that were revealed concerning harsh interactions with the deviated and the unworthy.

Here, we mention verses from both cases as examples:

The First Category: Verses related to the Prophet's calm and positive interactions with people.

«فاصبر» (The verse under discussion).

«و اخفض جناحك لمن اتّبعك من المؤمنین» (166) Lower your wing for the believers who follow you.

«و صلّ علیهم انّ صلاتك سكن لهم» (167) Send blessings upon them (and pray), for your prayer is a source of tranquility for them.

«اُدع الى سبیل ربّك بالحكمة و الموعظة الحسنة» (168) Invite people to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good counsel.

«فاعف عنهم و استغفر لهم و شاورهم» (169) Pardon them, ask forgiveness for them, and consult with them.

«اِدفع بالتى هى احسن» (170) Repel evil with what is better.

«تَعالَوا الى كلمة سواء بیننا و بینكم» (171) Come to a word that is common between us and you.

«فبایعهن و استغفر لهن» (172) Accept their pledge and ask forgiveness for them (from Allah).

«قل اذن خیر لكم» (173) Say: His listening is better for you.

«اذا جاءك الذّین یؤمنون بآیاتنا فقل سلام علیكم» (174) When those who believe in Our signs come to you, say to them: Peace be upon you.

«لو كنتَ فظّاً غلیظ القلب لانفضّوا من حولك» (175) If you had been harsh and hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from around you.

«و ان جنحوا للسلم فاجنح لها» (176) If (the enemies) incline toward peace, then incline toward it too.

«بشّر المؤمنین بان لهم من اللّه فضلاً كبیراً» (177) Give glad tidings to the believers that for them is a great bounty and reward from Allah.

The Second Category: Verses related to decisive conduct with the unworthy who are not amenable to guidance.

«فاعرض عنهم» (178) Turn away from them.

«فلا تطع المكذبین ودّوا لو تدهن فیدهنون» (179) Do not obey the deniers, who wish that you would compromise so that they would compromise. (A compromise accompanied by deviation from the path of truth.)

«جاهد الكفّار و المنافقین و اغلظ علیهم» (180) Strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be harsh upon them.

«هم العدو فاحذرهم» (181) They are the enemy, so beware of them.

«لئن لم ینته المنافقون و الذّین فى قلوبهم مرض و المرجفون فى المدینه لنغرینك لهم ثم لا یجاورونك فیها الا قلیلاً» (182) If the hypocrites, and those in whose hearts is a disease, and (also) those who spread false news and baseless rumors in Medina do not desist from their actions, We shall certainly incite you against them, and they will not be able to remain near you in this city except for a short while.

«لا تصلّ على احد منهم مات ابدا و لا تقم على قبره» (183) Never pray over any of them who dies, nor stand by his grave (for prayer and seeking forgiveness).

«ذرهم فى خوضهم یلعبون» (184) Leave them in their stubborn discussions to play.

«لا تقم فیه» (185) Never stand in it (Masjid al-Dirar) (for prayer).

«لا تطع الكافرین و المنافقین و دَع اذاهم» (186) Do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and disregard their annoyance.

Messages

1- Remembering the Divine power regarding the punishment and retribution of criminals is a factor for hope and patience. «بئس مثوى المتكبّرین فاصبر»

2- Delay in the retribution of criminals should not cause doubt or skepticism. «فاصبر ان وعداللّه حقّ»

3- The truthfulness of Divine promises is a source of patience and tranquility. «فاصبر» because «ان وعداللّه حقّ»

4- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله awaits the retribution of the disbelievers in this world, but Allah acts according to wisdom, and the Prophet's expectations do not disrupt His wisdom. «اما نرینّك او نتوفّینّك»

5- Patience in the face of hardships has no limit; one must be patient until the end of life. «فاصبر... امّا... نتوفینّك»

6- Allah is not hasty in punishing criminals. «امّا نرینك... او نتوفینّك»

7- It is not possible for criminals to receive full punishment in this world; another abode is necessary. «بعض الذّى نعدهم»

8- Divine affairs are timed, and each occurs at its appointed time. «نرینّك... او نتوفینّك»

9- Death is not annihilation; it is a transfer and transformation. «نتوفینّك»

10- Death is for everyone, even for the Prophets. «نتوفینّك»

11- No one will escape the power of Allah. «الینا یرجعون»

12- The summoning of individuals on the Day of Judgment is compulsory. «یُرجعون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِّن قَبْلِكَ مِنْهُم مَّن قَصَصْنَا عَلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُم مَّن لَّمْ نَقْصُصْ عَلَيْكَ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَن يَأْتِىَ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ قُضِىَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ (۷۸)
Certainly We have sent apostles before you. Of them are those We have recounted to you, and of them are those We have not recounted to you. An apostle may not bring any sign except by Allah’s permission. Hence, when Allah’s edict comes, judgment is made with justice, and it is thence that the falsifiers become losers. (78)

Key Points

Based on narrations, the number of Prophets is one hundred and twenty-four thousand, but in the Quran, only the names of twenty-six Prophets are mentioned: Adam, Noah, Idris, Salih, Hud, Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Joseph, Lot, Jacob, Moses, Aaron, Shu'ayb, Zachariah, John (Yahya), Jesus ('Isa), David, Solomon, Elias, Elisha (Al-Yasa'), Dhul-Kifl, Job (Ayyub), Jonah (Yunus), Uzayr, and Muhammad (peace be upon them all) [علیهم السلام].(187) One of the methods of education and guidance is relating the history of past generations and positive role models, and the Quran has employed this method repeatedly.

187) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam is the Seal of the Prophets (Khatam al-Anbiya). ("من قبلك" [before you] has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran, while "من بعدك" [after you] has not been mentioned even once).

2- Familiarity with the history of the Prophets is a source of solace for the Noble Prophet and a factor for patience. "فاصبر... منهم من قصصنا علیك" (So be patient... among them are those whose stories We have related to you).

3- Stories that are recited and pondered upon must be true (Haqq), and their purpose must also be the promotion of truth. "منهم من قصصنا علیك" (Among them are those whose stories We have related to you).

4- We should not underestimate storytelling. The Quran has narrated several stories. "قصصنا" (We have related).

5- Only He who sent the Prophets can narrate their history. "قصصنا - لم نقصص" (We have related - We have not related).

6- The Quran is not a history book; otherwise, it would have recounted the stories of all the Prophets. "منهم من لم نقصص" (Among them are those whose stories We have not related).

7- In relating history, the lessons (Ibrat) are important, not the statistics. "منهم من لم نقصص" (Among them are those whose stories We have not related).

8- We should not be upset if our names are not mentioned; the names of many Prophets have also not been mentioned. "منهم من لم نقصص" (Among them are those whose stories We have not related).

9- The miracles of the Prophets occur by Divine permission, not according to the expectations or whims of the people. "الا باذن اللّه" (Except by the permission of Allah).

10- Those who are indifferent toward the history and miracles of the Prophets will suffer loss. "قصصنا... یأتى بایة... خسر هنالك المبطلون" (We have related... he brings a sign... there the falsifiers will lose).

11- The final judgment between the Prophets and their opponents belongs to the Hereafter. "فاذا جاء امر اللّه قضى بالحقّ" (So when the command of Allah comes, He will judge with truth).

12- The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is the day of Allah's judgment and the shame of the followers of falsehood. "قضى بالحقّ و خسر هنا لك المبطلون" (He will judge with truth, and there the falsifiers will lose).

13- The real loss is the loss on the Day of Judgment. "خسر هنالك" (They will lose there).

14- Worse than the people of falsehood are those whose goal, besides following the path of falsehood, is to nullify and neutralize the efforts of the Prophets. "المبطلون" (The falsifiers/nullifiers).

Ayahs

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمَ لِتَرْكَبُوا۟ مِنْهَا وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۷۹)
It is Allah who created the cattle for you that you may ride some of them, and some of them you eat; (79)
وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ وَلِتَبْلُغُوا۟ عَلَيْهَا حَاجَةً فِى صُدُورِكُمْ وَعَلَيْهَا وَعَلَى ٱلْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ (۸٠)
and there are [numerous] uses in them for you, and that over them you may satisfy any need that is in your breasts, and you are carried on them and on ships. (80)
وَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ فَأَىَّ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ تُنكِرُونَ (۸۱)
He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny? (81)

Key Points

The best way to create and strengthen knowledge (of Allah) and gratitude is to pay attention to divine blessings. For this purpose, the best blessings are those that are visible and usable by the general public in every time and place. An'ām (أنعام) is the plural of Na'am (نَعَم), originally meaning camel, but it is also used to refer to the collective group of cattle, camels, and sheep. The benefits of livestock are not limited to providing transport and nutrition; their hides, intestines, wool, down, milk, and plowing are among other benefits. Even their waste products are used for natural fertilizer or as a means of generating heat. Furthermore, the role of animals in establishing factories for wool, spinning thread, leather production, and dairy is significant.

Messages

1- In the creation of existence, humanity is central. «جعل لكم الانعام... و لكم فیها منافع»

2- If livestock were not domesticated, human life would be paralyzed. Although their strength is greater than ours, Allah subdued them for us. «جعل لكم الانعام لتركبوا»

3- Islam has recommended the issue of nutrition and utilizing the meat of certain livestock, and raw consumption is not praised by Islam. «و منها تأكلون»

4- The blessings of livestock are not limited to what we utilize. (The word manāfi' [منافع - benefits] is indefinite [in the Arabic text]).

5- Man is dependent on animals «لتبلغوا علیها حاجة فى صدوركم» (That you may reach upon them a need in your breasts) (but the animal is not dependent on man).

6- The possibility of sea transport by ship is another divine blessing. «و علیها و على الفلك تحملون»

7- In the divine worldview, all utilization and blessings are a prelude to recognition (of Allah) and servitude. «آیات اللّه»

8- Man is ungrateful; he benefits from the blessings but denies the Owner of the blessing. «فاىّ آیات اللّه تنكرون»

9- The signs of knowing Allah are exposed to the view of everyone. «فاىّ آیات اللّه تنكرون»

Ayah

أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوٓا۟ أَكْثَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ قُوَّةً وَءَاثَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۸۲)
Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more numerous than them and were greater [than them] in power and with respect to the effects [they left] in the land. But what they used to earn did not avail them. (82)

Messages

1- Purposeful journeying (travel) is praised. «افلم یسیروا... فینظروا»

2- One of the Quran’s criticisms is the failure to journey for the sake of gaining experience, taking lessons, and acquiring knowledge. «افلم یسیروا... فینظروا»

3- History is one of the sources for knowledge and recognizing truths. «فینظروا كیف كان... من قبلهم»

4- The opposition of the disbelievers to the Prophet was due to their arrogance regarding their power and population; hence, the Quran states: We destroyed those who were stronger than them. «كانوا اكثر... و اشدّ»

5- Among the factors leading to the downfall and decline of civilizations is opposing the prophets and abandoning the Divine traditions (Sunan). «افلم یسیروا...»

6- Not every civilization or progress is a criterion for salvation and felicity. «قوّةً و آثاراً فى الارض فما اغنى عنهم...»

7- Preserving the relics of past nations is necessary for the lessons of future generations. «فینظروا»

8- In our calculations, we should not look only at the present state of individuals and societies; we must be mindful of the outcome (end result). «كیف كان عاقبة...»

9- Population and power are not barriers against Divine wrath. «اكثر منهم و اشدّ قوةً»

10- Human civilization is not constantly progressing; some past nations were more civilized. «قبلهم كانوا اشدّ منهم قوّةً و آثاراً»

11- Whatever we acquire is futile against Divine wrath. «فما اغنى عنهم ما كانوا یكسبون»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَرِحُوا۟ بِمَا عِندَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۸۳)
When their apostles brought them manifest proofs, they boasted about the knowledge they possessed, but they were besieged by what they used to deride. (83)
فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا۟ بَأْسَنَا قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَحْدَهُۥ وَكَفَرْنَا بِمَا كُنَّا بِهِۦ مُشْرِكِينَ (۸٤)
Then, when they sighted Our punishment, they said, ‘We believe in Allah alone, and disavow what we used to take as His partners.’ (84)

Key Points

Throughout human history, individuals, due to sciences that lead to civilization, progress, and wealth acquisition, or due to knowledge they possess about their beliefs inherited from their ancestors and tribes, consider themselves independent of revelation and the prophets. Indeed, people of limited capacity become arrogant as soon as they learn a few words or attain a degree or position. The phrase "نصیباً من الكتاب" (a portion of the Book) appears three times in the Qur'an, meaning having knowledge of only a part of the Book. In all three instances, individuals fell into deviation because they did not possess sufficient knowledge, but merely a portion of it. In one place, He says: Those who have a portion of knowledge turn their backs on the truth (188). In another place, He says: They purchased error instead of the truth (189). And in yet another Ayah, He says: This group believes in idols and Taghut (tyrants) instead of believing in Allah and the Messenger (190). In any case, limited knowledge exposes a person to arrogance, self-loss, and selling one's religion. In this Ayah too, He says: Scant knowledge prevents the acceptance of truth. "فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم" (They rejoiced in what they had of knowledge). The Qur'an describes three stages for knowledge: A) Beneficial knowledge, which leads to man's spiritual growth, just as Moses said to Khidr علیهما السلام: "May I follow you so that you may teach me some of the guidance you have been taught?" (191). B) Useless knowledge, such as knowing the exact number of the Companions of the Cave—whether they were three, five, or seven—because what was important was their decision to preserve their religion (192). C) Harmful knowledge, such as the knowledge of sorcery and magic used to cause separation between a man and his wife (193).

188) Al ‘Imran, 23. 189) An-Nisa, 44. 190) An-Nisa, 51. 191) Al-Kahf, 66. 192) Al-Kahf, 22. 193) Al-Baqarah, 102.

Messages

1- Sending the prophets to complete the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah) is one of the Divine traditions. "جاءتهم رسلهم" (Their messengers came to them).

2- The prophets went to the people. "جاءتهم رسلهم" (Their messengers came to them).

3- Academic arrogance prevents the acceptance of truth. "جاءتهم رسلهم بالبینات فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم" (Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they rejoiced in what they had of knowledge).

4- Human sciences and experiences cannot replace Divine teachings or render man independent of the prophets. "جاءتهم... بالبینات... عندهم من العلم" (They came to them... with clear proofs... what they had of knowledge).

5- The prophets are a proof (Hujjah) even for the scholars. "جاءتهم رسلهم بالبینات فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم" (Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they rejoiced in what they had of knowledge).

6- Divine wrath and punishment follow the completion of the proof. "جاءتهم... بالبینات... حاق بهم..." (They came to them... with clear proofs... encompassed them...).

7- All prophets possessed miracles. "جائتهم رسلهم بالبینات" (Their messengers came to them with clear proofs).

8- There is no escape route for the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment. ("حاق بهم" - It encompassed them).

9- Being arrogant about one's own knowledge leads to the belittling and mockery of Divine promises. "عندهم من العلم... یستهزؤن" (What they had of knowledge... they used to mock).

10- Joy is worthless if it prevents a person from traversing the path of perfection. "فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم... یستهزؤن" (They rejoiced in what they had of knowledge... they used to mock).

11- One of Allah's traditions is supporting the prophets by sending down wrath and punishment upon the disbelievers. "حاق بهم ما كانوا یستهزؤن" (What they used to mock encompassed them).

12- Allah's wrath is due to the actions of the people themselves. "حاق بهم ما كانوا یستهزؤن" (What they used to mock encompassed them).

13- Today's mockery leads to tomorrow's comprehensive wrath. "و حاق بهم ما كانوا یستهزؤن" (And what they used to mock encompassed them).

14- Unworthy actions turn into Divine wrath when they are continuous. "كانوا یستهزؤن" (They used to mock).

15- The logic of the opponents against the proof and miracle of the prophets is ridicule and mockery. "رسلهم بالبینات... یستهزؤن" (Their messengers with clear proofs... they used to mock).

16- Faith during distress is worthless, even if it is genuine. "فلمّا رأوا بأسنا قالوا آمنّا" (But when they saw Our might, they said, 'We believe').

17- Some groups do not believe until they see the Divine wrath. "فلما رأوا بأسنا قالوا آمنّا" (But when they saw Our might, they said, 'We believe').

18- Polytheism (Shirk) has no foundation. Even the polytheists disbelieve in their own polytheism (when facing reality). "كفرنا بما كنا به مشركین" (We disbelieve in what we used to associate with Him).

Ayah

فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنفَعُهُمْ إِيمَـٰنُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا۟ بَأْسَنَا ۖ سُنَّتَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى قَدْ خَلَتْ فِى عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸٥)
But their faith was of no benefit to them when they sighted Our punishment—Allah’s precedent, which has passed among His servants, and it is thence that the faithless will be losers. (85)

Key Points

During the time of Mutawakkil al-Abbasi, a Christian man committed adultery (zina) with a Muslim woman. Just as they were about to implement the prescribed punishment (hadd), the man converted to Islam. Yahya ibn Aktham said: His faith solved the problem, and the hadd is not necessary. A discussion ensued among them; Mutawakkil wrote a letter to Imam al-Hadi عليه السلام and presented the issue. The Imam said: «یضرب حتّى یموت» (He shall be struck until he dies). Since a disbelieving man assaulted a Muslim woman, his punishment is execution (qatl). The scholars objected to the Imam's answer and said: There is no such ruling (fatwa) in the Quran or the Sunnah. Mutawakkil wrote another letter to Imam al-Hadi عليه السلام requesting evidence and documentation. In response, the Imam wrote, citing this Ayah: «فلمّا رأوا بأسنا قالوا آمنّا باللّه... فلم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم» (When they saw Our might, they said, "We believe in Allah..." ...but their faith could not benefit them): Since that Christian man embraced faith after conviction and at the time of punishment, his faith is useless, and the hadd is not waived for him.(194)

194) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Distress brings the innate nature (fitrah) to bloom and leads disbelievers toward faith, but compulsory faith has no value. «فلم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم»

2- Faith must be voluntary, not out of compulsion or necessity. «فلم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم»

3- Relating the destiny of the disbelievers is a lesson for future generations. «قالوا آمنا... لم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم»

4- Divine punishments and rewards are governed by law. «رأوا بأسنا... سنّت اللّه...»

5- The ineffectiveness of compulsory faith is among the Divine traditions (Sunan). «سنّت اللّه التى...» (The tradition of Allah is the same for all individuals and societies. «و لن تجد لسنة اللّه تبدیلا»(195))

6- At the moment of death, man's loss becomes manifest. «خسر هنالك»

7- Compulsory faith that leaves no opportunity for action is a loss. «قالوا آمنّا... خسر هنالك الكافرون»

8- Seven verses ago, we read: «خسر هنالك المبطلون» (The falsifiers lost there), and here we read: «خسر هنالك الكافرون» (The disbelievers lost there). It becomes clear that the disbelievers are people of falsehood (Ahl al-Batil), and the people of falsehood are the disbelievers. 195) Al-Ahzab, 62; Fatir, 43 and Al-Fath, 23.
